claims.statement: CONFIG MECHANISM (why the extraction recorded cfg=None): this machine takes NO config block pointer. The only engine parameter is r4 = group-4 stream SLOT BASE, seeded by each wrapper with `li r4, 0` and stored by phase 0 into the stream cursor +0x6ea; both families pass 0. All other per-family variation comes from (a) the wrapper's pre-dispatch aim-recovery decay of the part-2 aim shorts +0x18e0/+0x18e2 (family A: *0.9 float round-trip; family B: arithmetic >>1), and (b) per-actor data the engine loads that the ctor/loader bound family-specifically: movement page +0x4ac (gravityFall +0x6c consumed at 800f45dc-45e4; turn-rate row +0xac via zz_006d144_/zz_006e1ac_; range rows: actor+0x868..+0x888 copied from page+0x134..+0x154 by the deploy init chunk_0007.c:32), the group-4 anim bank +0x1d80 (ctor-bound, family-specific stream content for slots 0-3), and the aim pitch +0x18da written by the aim subsystem zz_006e1d0_(actor,mask,part=1,clamp 0x4000) into the stride-6 record base +0x18d4 (+0x18d4+1*6 = +0x18da). The machine is config-BLOCK-less by design; the r4 integer is the whole "config".

claims.statement: +0x18da SEMANTICS: it is the PART-1 AIM PITCH short (BAM16) — element 1 of the stride-6 aim-record array based at +0x18d4. Writer: zz_006e1d0_ @0x8006e1d0 (via the wrapper zz_006e1ac_(actor, mask, part) = zz_006e1d0_(actor, mask, part, 0x4000)); with a lock target it computes desired = clamp(−(s16)actor+0x70 − FUN_800452a0(muzzleY_delta, horizDist), ±0x4000) from target pos +0x518 minus the per-part muzzle row (actor + part*0x30 + 0x8e0/0x8f0/0x900), steps toward it at page-turn-rate × (f32)actor+0x768 per tick, and returns 1 when reached / 0 stepping / −1 no-target (no-target path decays the record by FLOAT_804376d8 = 0.9). The engine turns this pitch into launch velocity: hSpeed(+0x44) = 40·cos(pitch), yVel(+0x48) = −40·sin(pitch), recomputed every tick in phases 2 and 3 (homing while the +0x1d10 window is open) and frozen at phase-3→4. FUN_800452a0 is un-decompiled (LIKELY atan2-shape from its (dy, dist) argument pair); the exact sign convention of the pitch is therefore LIKELY, but the formula and addresses are exact.

claims.statement: PORT SHAPE (mirror of shared-x-special.ts for this machine — suggested families/shared-aerial-dive-x.ts): export const SHARED_AERIAL_X = { SETUP_TIMER: 60.0 /*FLOAT_80439090*/, LAUNCH_TIMER: 20.0 /*FLOAT_80439094*/, DESCEND_TIMER: 60.0 /*FLOAT_80439090 reused at P3→4*/, REPOSITION_SCALE: 0.95 /*FLOAT_804390b0*/, STREAM_RATE: -1.0 /*FLOAT_8043907c*/, LAUNCH_SPEED: 40.0 /*FLOAT_804390e4*/, GRAVITY_PARAM: 1.0 /*FLOAT_804390d4*/, STEER_DECAY: 0.9 /*FLOAT_804390a0*/, SKID_DECEL: 0.95 /*FLOAT_804390b0 via zz_006ed8c_*/, SKID_ENTER_SPEED: 10.0 /*FLOAT_804390dc*/, EXIT_SPEED: 5.0 /*FLOAT_804390e8*/, FX_DIST_GATE: 250.0 /*FLOAT_804390ac*/ } as const. interface SharedAerialXConfig { slotBase: number /* r4 seed for +0x6ea; 0 for both known families */; aimRecover: { kind: 'mul', k: 0.9 } | { kind: 'shift', n: 1 } /* wrapper pre-decay of part-2 aim shorts +0x18e0/+0x18e2; A=mul 0.9, B=shift 1 */; whiffFrames?: number /* TUNED stand-in for the stream-end advance out of phase 2, same approximation gred.ts documents */ }. Host/actor hooks needed beyond the existing StreamContext: (1) aimPitch read+seek — part-1 record +0x18da with seek-toward-target clamp ±0x4000 at page-rate×dt and no-target 0.9 decay (phases 0/1/2, and 3 while tracking window open); (2) rangeCheck(prevActionIndex) using rows [1000,1000,2500] (identical for pl0400/pl040a/pl0406) × actor scale, driving the +0x541 lock-invalidate; (3) gravityFall from the movement page (−0.6 for all three borgs) written to gravityCoeff at P3→4; (4) landed() ground-snap boolean (zz_00677b0_ return) for P4; (5) yaw-only ground physics + dt-corrected decel 0.95 for P6 (already ported for the melee-lunge machine); (6) upper-cue latch 0x2c on landing (clears frozen bit 0x40, +0x87=0, +0x69c=0); (7) optional onEffect(subtype 0|2) FX hook; (8) exits: air-fall (zz_006a5a4_) on P4 timeout, ground-idle (zz_006a474_) at P6 — and NO attack-cooldown seed (+0x694 untouched, unlike zz_00ff2bc_). Registration: wire as actionTable[2] for family ctors 0x80074940 (pl0400, pl040a) and 0x801854f8 (pl0406); per-tick handler must ALSO apply cfg.aimRecover to +0x18e0/+0x18e2 before phase dispatch (that is the wrapper body).
evidence: structure mirrors packages/combat/src/families/shared-x-special.ts:26-48 (SHARED_X consts + SharedXConfig interface + createSharedXSpecial factory)
every constant and hook traced in claims M3/C1/C4/S1; whiffFrames approximation precedent: shared-x-special.ts:43-47 and families/gred.ts

claims.statement: UNRESOLVED detail: the airborne phase-0 stream start passes header args (10, 2) instead of (−1, −1) to zz_004beb8_ → zz_004c050_; these two words are forwarded into the stream-header opcode handlers (PTR_FUN_802d0da0) before playback starts, and the same (10,2) pair appears at many other air-start call sites corpus-wide (e.g. chunk_0010.c:2614, chunk_0018.c:3851). Their exact effect (start-frame/blend-mode override is the working hypothesis) is not yet decoded; the port's startStream can accept an optional headerArgs=(10,2) passthrough and treat it as a no-op until the opcode consuming it is decoded.

claims.statement: GAMEPLAY SHAPE (synthesis for the orchestrator): this shared X is an AIMED AERIAL DIVE/LEAP SHOT machine, distinct from zz_00ff2bc_ (blink-melee) and zz_00fed6c_ (melee lunge). Sequence: P0 blink-away windup + stream A (air variant only changes header args); P1 hold up to 60f until facing target; P2 aim window — on the stream's contact byte, launch at 40 u/f along the part-1 aim pitch with zero decel/gravity; P3 20f minimum powered flight with stream-driven homing window (+0x1d10, canceled if hit via +0x1d9), then on the stream's +0x1b01 latch switch to stream B with the borg's own fall gravity (−0.6); P4 ballistic descend up to 60f — land (stream C + cue 0x2c + FX) or air-fall exit; P5/P6 landing skid with 0.95 decay, stream D, ground-idle exit below speed 5. Both member families are pl04xx gun-borg families (pl0400/pl040a and pl0406) whose other actions route to the same 0x800f2xxx-0x800f3xxx shared-shooter engine cluster (zz_00f1e30_/zz_00f2374_/zz_00f2764_/zz_00f3028_/zz_00f33f4_/zz_00f3738_), so the X machine reads like the shooter cluster's 'jump-jet dive attack'.
evidence: phase transcriptions in M3; family action tables in W1; cluster membership: chunk_0010.c FUN_80074d08→zz_00f1e30_(actor,&DAT_802d4ea8), FUN_80074ea8/ecc/ef0/f14→zz_00f2374_/zz_00f2764_/zz_00f3028_/zz_00f3738_; chunk_0046.c FUN_80185784/7a8/7cc/7f0→zz_00f2374_/zz_00f2764_/zz_00f33f4_/zz_00f3738_

openQuestions: Header args (10,2) passed by the airborne phase-0 stream start: which PTR_FUN_802d0da0 header opcode consumes zz_004c050_'s param_5/param_6, and what do 10/2 mean (start-frame? blend?).
FUN_800452a0(dy, dist) body (the pitch solver used by zz_006e1d0_) — presumed atan2; needed only to pin the +0x18da sign convention for perfect launch-direction fidelity.
+0x760 writer/semantics (the 250.0 FX gate in phase 2) — un-located; FX-only impact.

+0x1b01 exact writer inside the stream VM (consumed as segment-complete latch by phase 3 and by zz_004c050_'s part-clock accumulation) — the port needs a stream-end signal stand-in until group-4 banks are byte-loaded (same whiffFrames approximation as gred.ts/shared-x-special.ts).

FUN_800b26cc / FUN_800b2ce4 (the zz_00b2190_ FX child's update callbacks) — unread; only needed if the FX child turns out to deal damage (its spawn copies target lock +0xcc, so worth a 15-minute read before declaring it cosmetic).
Group-4 stream slot CONTENT for banks 0x802c0980 (family A) and 0x8035c7c0 (family B) slots 0-3 (durations, contact-byte timing) — determines real phase-2 whiff timing and phase lengths.
findings.portImplications: Port this as a second parameterized shared machine alongside shared-x-special.ts (suggested packages/combat/src/families/shared-aerial-dive-x.ts). Key differences from the zz_00ff2bc_ port to respect: (1) the config is NOT a block — it is {slotBase: 0, aimRecover} where aimRecover is the wrapper's per-tick decay of the part-2 aim shorts (+0x18e0/+0x18e2): ×0.9 for pl0400/pl040a, ×0.5 (>>1) for pl0406; apply it in the factory-returned handler before phase dispatch, exactly where the ROM wrapper runs. (2) Four sequential group-4 stream slots (slotBase+0..3) instead of one, with the airborne fork changing only header args (10,2) — safe to ignore until decoded. (3) Launch velocity is DERIVED EVERY TICK from the part-1 aim pitch (+0x18da): hSpeed=40·cos(p), yVel=−40·sin(p), with a stream-gated homing window (+0x1d10, canceled by damage byte +0x1d9); the bridge needs an aimPitch field seeded by a seek-toward-target with ±0x4000 clamp (a lockPitch analog of the existing lockYaw). (4) Gravity is OFF during launch (gravityCoeff=0) and becomes the borg page's gravityFall (−0.6 for all three members) only at the P3→4 transition — reuse movementData's gravityFall export directly. (5) NO +0x694 cooldown seed at exit (do not copy shared-x-special's 4.0+dt), exits are plain zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_ analogs plus the cue-0x2c latch (clear frozen bit 0x40) on landing. (6) The phase-0 range check (rows 1000/1000/2500 × actor scale, indexed prevActionIndex%3, identical for all three borgs) only decides whether the lock is dropped (+0x541) — with no target the machine still runs, it just flies straight at current yaw/pitch; model it as lock invalidation, not a move gate. Registration: actionTable[2] for ctor families 0x80074940 (pl0400, pl040a) and 0x801854f8 (pl0406). P5's same-tick fall-through into P6 matters for frame parity (the skid stream starts and the decel/exit check still runs that tick). The +0x868 range-row writer open question from the ninja decode is now CLOSED (chunk_0007.c:31-38: page+0x134..+0x154 → actor+0x868..+0x888) — movementData.ts can export the three range rows for any future machine that needs them.
findings.summary: Engine 0x800f41c4 is a 7-phase shared AERIAL DIVE-SHOT X machine (phase table 0x8031b92c, phases 0x800f4214/4340/43f8/44e0/4610/4730/47a4) used by the pl04xx shooter families A (ctor 0x80074940, pl0400/pl040a, wrapper FUN_80074f38) and B (ctor 0x801854f8, pl0406, wrapper FUN_80185814). The cfg=None mystery is solved: the machine takes NO config block — its only parameter is r4 = group-4 stream slot base (both wrappers emit `li r4,0`, engine passes r4 untouched into phase 0 which stores it to the stream cursor +0x6ea), and the only other per-family code is the wrapper's aim-recovery decay of part-2 aim shorts +0x18e0/+0x18e2 (A: ×0.9, B: >>1). Machine shape: blink-away windup (motion=(pos−target)×0.95) + stream slot 0 → face target (≤60f) → aim window; on stream contact byte +0x1cef launch at 40 u/f along part-1 aim pitch +0x18da (hSpeed=40·cos, yVel=−40·sin, zero gravity) → ≥20f homing flight (window +0x1d10, damage byte +0x1d9 cancels), then stream slot 1 + gravityCoeff = page gravityFall (−0.6 all three borgs) → ballistic descend ≤60f → land (stream slot 2, cue 0x2c, FX) or air-fall exit zz_006a5a4_ → skid (slot 3, decel 0.95) → ground-idle exit zz_006a474_ below speed 5. No +0x694 cooldown. All constants DOL-read; wrapper→engine bl instructions, ctor→virtuals[0]→actionTable[2] chain, and per-borg page values (range rows 1000/1000/2500, turn rate 2560) fully derived; also closed the '+0x868 range-row writer' open question (chunk_0007.c:31-38 copies page+0x134..+0x154).

verdicts.note: Re-derived from decomp (organized/game/unsorted/800f41c4_zz_00f41c4.c:6-9) and fresh disasm 0x800f41c4-0x800f4210: lbz r0,0x541; extsb.; beq; li r0,0; stw r0,0xcc; lbz r0,0x540; lis r5,-0x7fce/addi r5,-0x46d4 = 0x8031b92c; lwzx r12; mtctr; bctrl. Only r0/r5/r12 (plus r1/lr/cr0) touched before bctrl; r4 never written, so the wrapper's li r4,0 reaches phase 0's stb r4,0x6ea(r3) at 0x800f423c.

Independent dol.py dump: 0x8031b92c..0x8031b944 = 0x800f4214/0x800f4340/0x800f43f8/0x800f44e0/0x800f4610/0x800f4730/0x800f47a4; 0x8031b948..0x8031b95c all zero. Max phase 6 confirmed.

Every store, branch, call, and float operand re-derived from raw disasm of 0x800f4214-0x800f484c this session. All 12 constants DOL-verified (e.g. -0x5990(r2)=0x80439090=60.0, -0x598c=0x80439094=20.0, -0x593c=0x804390e4=40.0, -0x5974=0x804390ac=250.0, -0x5944=0x804390dc=10.0, -0x5938=0x804390e8=5.0, -0x5980=0x804390a0=0.9, -0x5970=0x804390b0=0.95, -0x59a4=0x8043907c=-1.0, -0x59a0 dbl=0x43300000_80000000). Key stores match: 800f423c stb r4,0x6ea; 800f427c/4280/4284-4288 invalidate path (+0x541=1, +0xcc=0, +0x5ac=+0x5ae); 800f42f4-4304 air fork (10,2)/(-1,-1) on +0x5e0 bit 0x40 (rlwinm MB=ME=25) with slot unchanged; 800f4488/44a8 launch velocity 40*cos/-40*sin(+0x18da) with fneg at 800f4494; 800f45a0 +0x558=0.0 then +0x1b01 gate; 800f45dc-45e4 gravity page +0x6c -> +0x50; 800f46c0 li r4,0x2c cue; 800f4718 bl zz_006a5a4_, 800f4838 bl zz_006a474_; 800f478c P5 falls through via bl zz_00f47a4_. Helper semantics all re-verified (zz_006d144_->FUN_800669d0->FUN_80066a30 returns 1 when |+0x5aa|<=step, step=page[+0xac+(mask&0xf)*2]*dt, mask 0x40->+0x5ae 0x80->+0x72; zz_0066530_ 0x2c path +0x5e4=0x2c,+0x87=0,+0x69c=0.0 (FLOAT_804375d0 DOL-read 0), +0x5e0&=0xffffff3f; zz_006ed8c_ dt-corrected decay of +0x44/+0x48 with FLOAT_80437670=1.0; zz_0045238_/45204_ = cos/sin via FLOAT_80437078=9.58738e-05). No store to +0x694 anywhere in the range. Slot sequence base+0/+1/+2/+3 at P0/P3/P4-landing/P5 with +0x6ea auto-increment confirmed at 800f4308-4320, 800f45e8-45f4, 800f46a8-46b4, 800f4774-4780. Nit: the P0 parenthetical 'moves ~95% of the way PAST twice' is muddled prose; the actual op is +0x38=(pos-lockTargetPos), *=0.95, pos+=, i.e. a blink AWAY from the target, correctly transcribed.

Both wrappers seed li r4,0 (80074f44, 8018581c disasm-verified) and phase 0 consumes it at 800f423c stb r4,0x6ea; engine dispatch passes r4 through untouched (M1). zz_006e1d0_ writes *(short*)(actor + part*6 + 0x18d4) (organized/.../8006e1d0_*.c), so part1->+0x18da, part2 record base +0x18e0 and +2 -> +0x18e2. FUN_80066838 semantics confirmed (-1 if +0xcc==0; 1 if +0x1dd&0x80; else clz-based 0/1 on dist vs range*(f32)+0xb4) — with one omitted detail: the Y component of (+0x518 - +0x20) is zeroed (local_24=FLOAT_804375d0=0.0) before PSVECMag, so the check is HORIZONTAL distance. Anim-bank binding verified: ctor A stw 0x802c0980->+0x1d80 (80074a24), ctor B stw 0x8035c7c0->+0x1d80 (801855dc); zz_004beb8_ resolves group/slot bytes off +0x1d80 and forwards to zz_004c050_.

Full disasm 0x80074f38-0x80074fbc re-derived: 80074f44 li r4,0; 80074f50 lfs f1,-0x7228(r2)=0x804377f8 (DOL 0x3f666666=0.9); 80074f40 lfd f2,-0x7220(r2)=0x80437800 (DOL 0x43300000_80000000); int->float->*0.9->fctiwz->sth round-trips on +0x18e0 (80074f54-80) and +0x18e2 (80074f84-a8); 80074fac 4807f219 bl 0x800f41c4. Everything independently re-derived. DOL dumps: 0x802d4e38[0]=0x80074ccc, 0x8035d0e0[0]=0x801856e8, root tables [0x80074d08,0x80074e48,0x80074f38] and [0x80185724,0x80185724,0x80185814]; index 2 = X wrapper. My own byte-scan of all DOL data sections: 0x80074f38 appears only at 0x802d4ea4, 0x80185814 only at 0x8035d128. Dispatcher decomp verified at chunk_0010.c ~1951-1959 and chunk_0046.c ~255-263. Per-tick virtuals verified: FUN_80074ae8 resets +0x144..+0x148 when +0x774>=+0x78e and decays +0x18da/+0x18e0/+0x18e2 by FLOAT_804377f8=0.9 when (+0x5e0&3)==0; FUN_80185618 sets +0x58e|=1, +0x748=2, same x0.9 decay via FLOAT_8043af40 (DOL-read 0x3f666666=0.9).

All facts verified: zz_006e1ac_ passes clamp 0x4000; zz_006e1d0_ writes *(short*)(actor+part*6+0x18d4), muzzle row at actor+part*0x30+0x8e0/0x8f0/0x900, desired = clamp(-(s16)+0x70 - FUN_800452a0(muzzleYdelta, dist)), step = pageTurnRate x (f32)+0x768, no-target decay FLOAT_804376d8=0.9 (DOL-read), returns 1/0/-1; engine consumption at 800f4478-44a8 and 800f4530-4560 with fneg on sin. ONE CORRECTION that strengthens the claim: FUN_800452a0 is NOT un-decompiled — it is in chunk_0005.c: `return (int)(short)(FLOAT_8043707c * (float)gnt4_atan2_bl(param_1,param_2))` with FLOAT_8043707c DOL-read = 10430.3779 = 65536/2pi. The atan2 hypothesis is thus CONFIRMED, not just LIKELY: pitch = clamp(-(s16)+0x70 - atan2BAM(dy, horizDist), +/-0x4000). Also note zz_006e1d0_ zeroes the vector's Y (FLOAT_80437658 DOL-read = 0.0) before PSVECMag, confirming the 'horizDist' reading.

Synthesis verified against re-derived facts: all 12 named constants match DOL reads and their usage sites; slotBase/aimRecover config matches wrapper disasm (A: mul 0.9 round-trip, B: srawi 1); hooks 1-8 each trace to verified engine behavior; no +0x694 write confirmed; registration as actionTable[2] matches root-table index 2 for both ctors; the wrapper pre-decay of +0x18e0/+0x18e2 before phase dispatch matches. Structure mirrors shared-x-special.ts:26-48 (verified: SHARED_X consts, groundSlot/airSlot, onHit, whiffFrames precedent at :47 and :127). One imprecision: hook (6) says cue 0x2c 'clears frozen bit 0x40' — zz_0066530_ actually clears bits 0x40 AND 0x80 (+0x5e0 &= 0xffffff3f); M3 states this correctly as ~0xc0, so the port should clear both.

Verified as an appropriately-labeled UNRESOLVED: disasm 800f42e8-800f4308 shows rlwinm. extracting +0x5e0 bit 0x40 selecting r7=0xa/r8=2 vs r7=-1/r8=-1; zz_004beb8_ decomp forwards param_6/param_7 verbatim into zz_004c050_, whose opcode loop passes them as args 5/6 to every (*(&PTR_FUN_802d0da0)[bVar1])() header-opcode handler before playback. Other (10,2) call sites confirmed at chunk_0010.c:2614/2730, chunk_0018.c:3851, chunk_0029.c:3410. Effect of the pair genuinely not decoded statically; the proposed no-op passthrough is a safe port stance.

Gameplay synthesis is consistent with every verified phase transcription (blink-away P0, 60f face P1, contact-gated 40 u/f launch P2, 20f homing window P3 with +0x1d9 cancel, -0.6 gravity descend P4, skid P5/P6). extraFindings: FUN_800452a0 IS decompiled (chunk_0005.c), contrary to S1's aside: `int FUN_800452a0(double dy, double dist) { return (int)(short)(FLOAT_8043707c * (float)gnt4_atan2_bl(dy, dist)); }` with FLOAT_8043707c DOL-read = 10430.3779296875 = 65536/(2*pi). This CONFIRMS the atan2 hypothesis and pins the pitch formula exactly: desiredPitch = clamp(-(s16)actor+0x70 - atan2BAM(muzzleYdelta, horizDist), +/-0x4000). Only the physical sign convention (up vs down positive) remains untested.

FUN_80066838's range check is HORIZONTAL-only: after gnt4_PSVECSubtract_bl(+0x518, +0x20, &vec) it overwrites vec.y with FLOAT_804375d0 (DOL-read = 0.0) before gnt4_PSVECMag_bl. The port's rangeCheck(prevActionIndex) hook should compare horizontal (XZ) distance, not full 3D distance. Same Y-zeroing pattern appears in zz_006e1d0_ (FLOAT_80437658 = 0.0), confirming its 'horizDist' argument.

zz_0066530_ cue 0x2c clears BOTH +0x5e0 bits 0x40 and 0x80 (&= 0xffffff3f); P1's hook list mentions only bit 0x40. Its else-branch (any cue != 0x2c) does the inverse: +0x5e4=0x2d, +0x87=1, +0x5e0 |= 0x40 (no +0x69c write) — useful if other machines call it with different cues.

Ctor-to-borg membership is directly recorded in the project's own extraction: packages/combat/src/data/actionStreamTables.json /borgs maps pl0400 = CLAW ROBOT and pl040a = ISAAC to constructorAddress 0x80074940, and pl0406 = DEATH BORG OMEGA to 0x801854f8. The port can register by these names; this also closes the weakly-evidenced pl0406<->ctor-B link in C4.
zz_00b2190_ additionally stores the owner actor pointer at child+0x8c, sets child[0]=1 (alive flag) and child+0x164=0 — omitted from S3's field list; relevant if the FX child is ever ported.

chunk_0003.c:7690 also ORs bit 0x04 into +0x1d9 (guarded by `(+0x1d9 & 4)==0 && (+0x1da & 4)==0`), in addition to the 0x02/0x10/0x20/0x40/0x80 writers cited in S2 — so any nonzero test on +0x1d9 in phase 3 can also trip on that event class.

Return-value plumbing behind M3's phase-1 advance: zz_006d144_ is Ghidra-typed void, but disasm 0x8006d184-0x8006d1a4 shows it calls FUN_800669d0 last and restores only r0/r30/r31 before blr, so FUN_80066a30's 0/1 propagates untouched through both epilogues to the engine's cmpwi r3,0 at 800f43d0 — the 'returns 1 when aligned' reading is sound.

Family-A/B root action handlers echo the same aim-decay styles as their X wrappers: chunk_0010 FUN_80074d08 (family A action 0) does +0x18da >>= 1, and chunk_0046 FUN_80185724 (family B actions 0/1) does >>1 on all three of +0x18e0/+0x18e2/+0x18da before dispatching via +0x581 sub-table PTR_FUN_8035d12c — worth knowing when porting the rest of these families' action tables.

Organized-corpus paths cited in the claims all exist as named (phase files under game/battle/force-setup, game/combat/knockback, game/physics/movement, game/unsorted; wrapper mirrors 80074f38_FUN_80074f38.c and 80185814_FUN_80185814.c), and the Ghidra mirror of wrapper A indeed drops the li r4,0 — raw disasm was required, as the dig noted.

findings.portImplications: Port this as a SECOND parameterized shared machine alongside shared-x-special.ts — but with a crucial architectural difference the code comments must state: the ROM has NO per-family config block here (the scout's 'config 0x80325acc' is actually the global phase table). The TS config object is a port-side convenience derived from borgNumber, not a ROM structure. Concretely: (1) one createSeries3XSpecial(cfg, ctx) factory, wired as actionTable[2] AND actionTable[3] for all four families (pl0301; pl0302+pl0306; pl0303; pl0307+pl030d) — six borgs total, four ctors, one engine; (2) phase shapes are sharedXPhase0/1/2 minus the combo-cursor increment, with group 2 slots 0x10/0x11, face(0xc1) retained in ALL phases, and a groundSnap+pos-revert call each frame; (3) per-borg onHit map: 0x301 -> child(kind 2, sub 0x13); 0x302 -> blast(rec 0x1f, vel = speedVec*(5,5,5)); 0x303 -> ammoGate(slot 2, cost 1) ? blast(rec 0x3b) : commonExit-immediately (note: ammo failure CANCELS the move, a behavior shared-x has no analog for); 0x306 -> none; (4) 0x307/0x30d get the phase-2 repeating follow-up stream (slots 0x12/0x13) gated on contactP1>0 && ammoSlot2>0 — requires mirroring the +0x1cf0 contact byte and a raw ammo-count read into the bridge, both new host hooks; (5) two-tier cooldown (8.0 air-landing / 16.0 common exit) vs ninja's single 4.0 — RomActor needs no new fields for this, just the two constants; (6) the +0x1900/+0x18da meter decays are engine-visible but consumer-less in this dig — port them as tracked-but-unused actor scalars with an UNRESOLVED comment so the eventual consumer dig can light them up; (7) famB's companion spawn (zz_01d2ffc_, child kind 0x28) is OUTSIDE the phase machine and its trigger is untraced — do not wire it into the X phases; leave it as a documented residual like ninja's shuriken table host-wiring.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared X-machine 0x8010ce60 complete, all raw-disasm verified. Headline architecture finding: this machine is GLOBAL and CONFIG-LESS — 0x80325acc is its 3-entry PHASE table ([0x8010ceb0, 0x8010cfbc, 0x8010d0d0, NULL]), not a config block, and 0x80325ab8 is a 5-entry variant table whose entries are ALL 0x8010ce60. 'Handler' 0x8010cdec = the action-table entry (decays meter +0x1900 x0.96 then dispatches variantTable[+0x581]); 'wrapper' 0x8010ce60 = the phase dispatcher (+0x541 target-invalidation prologue, then phaseTable[+0x540]). No bl call sites exist for either; families reference 0x8010cdec directly in their root action tables. Per-family behavior lives in INLINE BORG SWITCHES on actor+0x3e8: phase-1 on-hit payloads (0x301 -> zz_00c3be0_(0x13); 0x302 -> zz_0082824_(0x1f); 0x303 -> ammo-gated zz_0082824_(0x3b) with no-ammo -> immediate exit) and a phase-2 repeating follow-up stream (slots 0x12/0x13) for 0x307/0x30d gated on contact byte +0x1cf0 and ammo count +0x784. Machine shape: P0 blink-reposition (motion=(pos-target)*0.95, group-2 stream slot 0x10/0x11, rate -1.0), P1 tick+face+reposition+hit-check, P2 gravity-1.0 recovery with descriptor-gravity restore on +0x1d0f, dual cooldowns 8.0 (air-landing, cue 7 via zz_006a750_) / 16.0 (common exit via zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_). Bonus: the engine serves FOUR families / six borgs — two Ghidra-missed ctors 0x80106e3c (pl0301) and 0x80107e7c (pl0302+pl0306) found by raw scan, plus the task's 0x8019c380 (pl0303) and 0x801ba020 (pl0307+pl030d); all four root dispatchers, vtables, and action-table words dumped as wrapper proof. Every float DOL-read; +0x1d10 and +0x558/+0x560 confirmed absent.

claims.statement: Port shape mirroring shared-x-special.ts — new shared machine 'shared-flight-x.ts' (knight-line lance charge), 6 phases. Interface: interface SharedFlightXConfig { groundSlot: number /* cfg+0x00, group-4 slot when grounded */; airSlot: number /* cfg+0x04, when +0x5e0&0x40 */; landSlot: number | null /* cfg+0x08, −1→null; group-4 landing anim (unused by all 5 shipped configs) */; reposScale: number /* cfg+0x0c: 0.95 or 0.92 */; flightTime: number /* cfg+0x10 raw; runtime = ×10, −10 per active frame */; extendBudget: number /* cfg+0x12 raw; runtime = ×10 */; extendRate: number /* cfg+0x14 = 8 */; extendRateFloor: number /* cfg+0x16 = 2 */; speed0: number /* cfg+0x18 = 0 */; speedMax: number /* cfg+0x1c = 30 */; accel: number /* cfg+0x20 = 2 per dt */; launchFrames?: number /* port approximation for the +0x1cee stream launch signal */; whiffFrames?: number }. Machine constants (analog of SHARED_X): REPOS handled per-config; STREAM_RATE −1.0; GRAVITY 1.0; DECAY 0.9; SPEED_EPSILON 0.5; HITSTOP_DT 0.25; WINDUP_TIMER 60.0; AIR_RECOVERY_TIMER 120.0; COOLDOWN 8.0. Host hooks beyond the existing StreamContext: (1) pitch channel — actor.steerYaw (+0x18da) steered toward lock target each frame (clamp ±0x4000, no-target decay ×0.9) with velocity projection hSpeed=speed×cos(pitch), yVel=−speed×sin(pitch); (2) face-button input test (mash/hold, mask 0xf0f00); (3) hover bit toggle (controlWord |= 0x40 on launch, &= ~0x40 on ground exit) + upper-cue byte 0x2d; (4) group-0 slot-13 fall stream (args 0x14,1) for the P4 airborne branch; (5) hitstop (dt=0.25 tick substitution) when contact byte +0x1cef reads negative; (6) trail/afterimage FX hooks (renderer-side no-ops OK). Registration: five families of configs — fam1 (pl0700/pl0709), fam2A (pl0701/pl0708), fam2B (pl070c/pl070d — the only intra-family borg switch, keyed on borgNumber), fam3 (pl0702/pl070a), fam4 (pl0705/pl070b); all wired as actionTable[2] with all 5 variants → same machine.

findings.portImplications: This is a genuinely different shared machine from zz_00ff2bc_ (3-phase melee blink) — it is a 6-phase pitch-steered FLYING LANCE CHARGE with button-fueled flight extension, so it needs its own shared module (shared-flight-x.ts), not a SharedXConfig reuse. Key deltas vs shared-x-special.ts: (1) config is 11 data fields, zero callbacks — per-family porting is literally a config object away, including the one borg-switched family (fam2: pl0701/0708 vs pl070c/070d get different reposScale 0.95/0.92 and extendBudget 30/20); (2) new host capability needed: a pitch channel on the actor (reuse actor.steerYaw = +0x18da) steered toward the lock target with ±0x4000 clamp and 0.9 no-target decay, projected as hSpeed=speed*cos(pitch), yVel=-speed*sin(pitch) with gravityCoeff forced 0 during flight and restored from the descriptor at exits; (3) new input hook: face-button activity test (mask 0xf0f00 on the pad word) driving the 8→2-decaying transfer from extendBudget into flightTime — expose as ctx.isExtendInputActive() so the bridge can wire real pad state; (4) hitstop: P4 ticks the stream with dt=0.25 when the contact byte is negative — the stream VM tick needs an optional dt override; (5) exits use cooldown 8.0 (not 4.0) and the P4-airborne branch starts the generic group-0 slot-13 fall stream (args 0x14,1) — same one the bridge already models for air-falls; (6) two port approximations required until stream banks are byte-loaded, both precedented: launchFrames (stands in for the +0x1cee windup-anim launch signal) and the flight-time drain gate +0x1b01 (safe to approximate as always-on during phase 3, making base flight = flightTime/10 frames + extension). Registration shape: all four families wire actionTable[2] → createSharedFlightX(cfg) with all 5 variants identical; 10 borgs (pl0700-070d knight line) land in one commit once the module exists.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared X-machine engine zz_0149708_ @0x80149708 (knight line, 4 families / 10 borgs, x-action 2) — a 6-phase pitch-steered flying lance charge, fully DERIVED. Phase table PTR_FUN_8033ed3c dumped (6 entries + NULL, bounded by the adjacent zz_0149ec8_ machine's table @0x8033ed58). All six phases transcribed with every float operand read from boot.dol: windup 60f (FLOAT_8043a384) with 0.95/0.92 blink-reposition, launch on stream signal +0x1cee loading an 11-field config (flight time ×10, button-extend budget ×10, transfer rate 8→floor 2, speed 0→30 @ accel 2/dt), flight phase projecting speed through steered pitch +0x18da (hSpeed=v·cos, yVel=−v·sin, homing via zz_006e1d0_ clamp ±0x4000), decel/impact phase with 0.9 decay, 0.5 speed epsilon, 0.25-dt hitstop on negative +0x1cef, and three exits (ground idle zz_006a474_, air-fall zz_006a5a4_ after 120f, landing zz_006a750_ cue 7) all seeding cooldown +0x694 = 8.0+dt. Config mechanism proven by raw disasm of all four wrappers (Ghidra dropped every r4 load): lis/addi → bl 0x80149708, configs 0x80308078 / 0x8032c814+0x8032c838 (family 2 is borg-switched on actor+0x3e8) / 0x8033c3d4 / 0x80373848, all five blocks dumped byte-exact and decoded — pure data, no callbacks. Port shape (SharedFlightXConfig) specified mirroring shared-x-special.ts. One drive-by correction: gred.ts:177's zz_00677b0_ return-sense comment is inverted.

The decompile (chunk_0009.c:3226) is gnt4_PSVECSubtract_bl((param_1+0x518), &local_48, &local_48) where local_48 preloaded from +0x910/+0x920/+0x930 (chan=1), and PSVECSubtract is dst = a − b (raw ps_sub decode of 0x8020ba78: f6 = [r3] − [r4]) — so dy = (+0x518).y − aimVec.y, i.e. '+0x518 minus the chan-1 aim vector', NOT 'aim vector minus +0x518' as claimed. This flips the sign of atan2BAM's dy argument, which flips desired-pitch sign — load-bearing for a port. CONFIRMED sub-facts: thunk passes 0x4000 (chunk_0009.c:3181); steers s16 at actor+chan*6+0x18d4; desired = clamp(−(+0x70) − atan2BAM(dy,|dxz|), ±0x4000) with y zeroed via FLOAT_80437658=0.0 before magnitude; step rate = descr(+0x4ac)[0xac+(mask&0xf)*2] × f32 +0x768; returns 1 reached / 0 stepping / −1 no-target with ×0.9 (FLOAT_804376d8) decay; engine call sites (0xc0,1) at all four phases confirmed.

Press-edge word ⇒ mash (per-press transfer), matching the claim's lean. Refinement the claim missed: the doc is NOT silent on 0xf0000 — pad-bit-binding-decode-2026-07-05.md:179 calls +0x5b4 & 0xf0000 the 'high direction byte' and :289-291 identifies bits 16-23 as a remapped direction/stick encoding (chunk_0008.c:3438 >>0x10 & 0xf). So the high nibble is direction bits, not unexplained virtual command bits — direction press-edges also count toward the extend transfer.

Design synthesis; every traced fact it rests on verified: SHARED_X analog structure and factory at shared-x-special.ts:26-48/155-175 (actionTable[2] convention documented there and in bridge.ts:517-521); all constants and config fields per MACHINE-3..9/CONFIG-2..6; whiffFrames/launchFrames approximation matches shared-x-special.ts:43-47; landSlot=−1 in all five shipped configs confirmed; borg groupings (fam1 pl0700/0709, fam2A pl0701/0708, fam2B pl070c/070d, fam3 pl0702/070a, fam4 pl0705/070b) corroborated by actionStreamTables.json ctor audit. One enrichment: since face calls use mask 0xc0, FUN_80066a30 writes BOTH +0x5ae and +0x72 while turning — +0x5ae is continuously steered during P0-P3, not a one-shot snapshot.

extraFindings: HELPER-1 correct reading (the one refutation): zz_006e1d0_ computes the steer vector as (actor+0x518) MINUS the chan-1 vector at +0x910/+0x920/+0x930 — PSVECSubtract convention dst = a − b proven by raw paired-single decode of gnt4-PSVECSubtract-bl @0x8020ba78 (psq_l f2,0(r3); psq_l f4,0(r4); ps_sub f6,f2,f4; psq_st f6,0(r5)). dy therefore = (+0x518).y − aim.y; a port using the claim's direction gets inverted pitch homing (climbs when it should dive).

zz_006d144_ facing-complete return independently derived (was only port-established): zz_006d144_ (chunk_0009.c:2497-2508) tail-calls FUN_800669d0 (chunk_0008.c:3374-3384) which tail-calls FUN_80066a30 (chunk_0008.c:3390-3424): returns 1 when |remaining yaw delta at +0x5aa| <= step, else steps and returns 0. Ghidra types both as void; the return value flows through r3 tail-calls — the P1 caller's `iVar3 != 0` test is real.

Yaw turn-rate formula (not in the claims, load-bearing for port fidelity): FUN_800669d0 computes step = s16 descriptor(+0x4ac)[0xac + (mask&0xf)*2] × dt(+0x1dc8). With mask 0xc0, (0xc0&0xf)=0 → descriptor turn-rate row 0 — the same +0xac row-0 slot the pitch steer reads, but yaw scales by dt while pitch scales by f32 +0x768.

Face mask 0xc0 semantics: FUN_80066a30 bit 0x40 writes +0x5ae (lock yaw) and bit 0x80 writes +0x72 (heading) every step — so during P0-P3 the flight direction +0x5ae continuously tracks the target (P3 integrates along it), giving horizontal homing in addition to the pitch homing; heading turns with it.

PAD-1 refinement: pad-bit-binding-decode-2026-07-05.md:179 and :289-291 identify +0x5b4/+0x5bc bits 16-23 as a remapped direction/stick encoding ('high direction byte', chunk_0008.c:3438 >>0x10 & 0xf) — the 0xf0000 nibble of the 0xf0f00 extend mask is direction press-edges, not unexplained virtual command bits. Flight extension therefore counts ABXY presses AND direction presses (popcount can exceed 1 per frame, transferring multiple xfer units); Dolphin check still worthwhile for the per-frame multiplicity.

Stage 1 halves two family-local charge-meter shorts then bl stage 2; stage 2 loads r4 = config block via lis/addi and bl the engine. claims.statement: Port shape mirroring shared-x-special.ts: module `shared-charge3.ts` (suggested) with consts SHARED_CHARGE3 = { WINDUP_TIME: 30.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae00 -> +0x560*/, STREAM_RATE: -1.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae04*/, HIT_FORWARD_SPEED: 10.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae14 -> +0x44*/, HIT_DECEL: -0.3 /*FLOAT_8043ae18 -> +0x4c*/, RECOVERY_GRAVITY: 1.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae1c*/ } and interface SharedCharge3Config { slot: number /*cfg u32[0], group-2 stream slot; 1 for both families*/; repositionScale: number /*cfg f32[+4] = 0.98; P0 blink scale AND P1/P2 per-tick motion decay*/; onHit: ((actor, ctx) => void) | null /*cfg ptr[+8]; fires once on the P2 +0x1cef contact transition, BEFORE the 10/-0.3 speed writes*/; }. 4 phases: P0 setup (phase++ FIRST, timer=30, face, zero motion+turn rates, blink motion=(pos-target)*scale then pos+=motion, ground snap, startStream(actor, 0xf, group 2, cfg.slot, rate -1.0), +0x80c=0); P1 windup (tick gated on part-anim-hold analog, motion*=scale glide, timer-=dt, advance on timer<=0 OR facing-complete — the ROM has NO whiff fallback need here since the timer IS the fallback); P2 ride (tick, face, motion*=scale, on contactP0>0: phase++, cfg.onHit?.(), hSpeed=10, hDecel=-0.3); P3 recovery (tick, afterimage on +0x1d10 analog, on +0x1d0f stream event reload gravityCoeff from family anim-bank value (runtime data — needs a TUNED fallback constant), integratePhysics(1.0, actor, heading+180deg) flying backward, on landing-contact clear control bits &~3 and exit grounded?idleReset:airFall). Host hooks needed beyond shared-x-special's set: reversed-heading integration, the +0x1b03 tick-hold gate, and a per-family animBankGravity fallback. Family wiring: wrap as actionTable[3]; on entry (the wrapper layer, NOT the engine) halve the two family charge meters — expose as an optional preDispatch hook { halveChargeMeters: true } since the offsets are family-local (+0x18e0/+0x18e2 vs +0x1922/+0x1926).

findings.portImplications: Port as a second parameterized shared machine alongside shared-x-special.ts (suggested families/shared-charge3.ts), wired as actionTable[3] for the pl0619 family and the pl061f/pl0625 family. Reuse existing rom/ primitives directly: startStream/tickStream (group 2, mask 0xf, rate -1.0), integratePhysics (with yaw = heading + 0x8000 — new: backward flight), vecSubtract/vecScale/vecAdd for the 0.98 blink+glide, dispatch exits already modeled by gred/ninja ports. Engine-level constants (30.0 windup, 10.0/-0.3 hit push, 1.0 recovery gravity) are DOL-derived and go in a module const; per-family config is only {slot:1, scale:0.98, onHit}. The onHit callbacks map cleanly onto the existing projectile pipeline: family 1 spawns 2x table-0x8035c1e0 kind-9 children (variant 1 for pl0619, ammo-gated via the already-ported zz_006dbe0_ cost gate = weapon-cell decrement); family 2 spawns 2x table-0x802d9758 kind-8 children (variants 17/18 for pl061f ammo-gated, 21/22 for pl0625 ungated) with initial velocity = owner speed x the 3-float row at 0x802d9794+variant*0x50 (dumpable from DOL for exact spawn vectors). Two approximations need labeling: the phase-1 tick gate (+0x1b03) can be approximated as always-tick (timer still bounds the phase), and the phase-3 anim-bank gravity reload needs a TUNED fallback until a Dolphin read pins blob+0x1b0+0x6c per borg. The charge-meter halving (+0x18e0/+0x18e2, +0x1922/+0x1926) belongs in the family wrapper layer, not the shared engine, mirroring the ROM split.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared charge-release engine zz_0177a3c_ @0x80177a3c (x-action THREE for the pl0619 family and the pl061f/pl0625 family) is complete with all five deliverables DERIVED. The engine dispatches a 4-entry phase table PTR_FUN_80352af8 = [0x80177a8c, 0x80177b88, 0x80177c48, 0x80177d08] with r4=config flowing untouched from each family wrapper (exhaustive bl-scan: exactly two callers). Machine shape: P0 blink-away setup (motion=(pos-target)x0.98 cfg scale, pos+=motion, 30.0-unit timer, group-2 stream slot 1 at rate -1.0), P1 timed windup glide (tick gated on +0x1b03, timer-=dt, advance on expiry or facing-complete), P2 attack ride (on +0x1cef contact: phase++, per-family callback, +0x44=10.0/+0x4c=-0.3 push), P3 backward-flying recovery (gravity 1.0, yaw=heading+180deg, +0x1d0f anim-bank gravity reload, +0x1d10 afterimage, +0x1cee exit to zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_; no +0x558/+0x694/zz_006a750_ anywhere). Config layout {u32 group-2 slot, f32 repositionScale, ptr onHit} proven from the engine's r31-relative loads; both families' configs are identical (slot 1, 0.98) except the callback: FUN_8012756c spawns paired kind-9 table-0x8035c1e0 children (pl0619 variant 1, ammo-gated) via zz_0182fcc_, FUN_8015bcb0 spawns paired kind-8 table-0x802d9758 children (pl061f 17/18 gated, pl0625 21/22 ungated) via zz_0084600_. Both wrappers halve family-local charge meters on entry — the charge-release signature — and are bound at index 3 of their family root action tables (dispatchers FUN_80127174/FUN_8015b7a0 indexing +0x580).

Design claim grounded entirely in the verified machine facts. Structure mirror verified against shared-x-special.ts (SHARED_X consts, SharedXConfig with slot/onHit, createSharedXSpecial factory wired as actionTable[2]). All five constants, the 3-field config, the phase-by-phase behavior list, the onHit-before-speed-writes ordering, the heading+180 recovery, the &~3 control-bit clear, the runtime animBankGravity gap (BSS-sourced +0x4ac->+0x6c value), and the family-local charge-meter offsets are each backed by claims I confirmed above. The P1 characterization (timer is the built-in fallback) matches the disassembled transition. Suggested naming/hook decisions are non-factual and out of verification scope.

All contrasts verified against shared-x-special.ts: actionTable[2] (line ~157) vs 3; STREAM group 4 (line 13) vs group 2 (li r5,2 @0x80177b58); +0x6ea combo-cursor write present in X-tap (line 96) and absent here (verified over the full disasm range); pooled 0.95 REPOSITION_SCALE (line 27) vs cfg-driven 0.98; 3-phase table (line 2) with no windup timer vs 4 phases + 30.0 timer; X-tap recovery integrates toward lockYaw (line 139, forward) vs heading+0x8000 here; charge-meter halving only in these wrappers. Shared elements (+0x541/+0xcc prologue, (pos-target)*k blink, +0x1cef hit callback, zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_ exits, +0x694 cooldown present in X-tap but absent here) all confirmed on both sides.

extraFindings: Family 2 action table @0x80345be8 differs structurally from family 1 beyond index 3: words [4] and [5] (0x80345bf8/0x80345bfc) are 0x00000000, and 0x80345c00..0x80345c08 hold 0x8015b83c/0x8015b914/0x8015b970 with 0x80345c0c=0x00040005 — i.e. NO default-stub padding to index 8 like family 1's 0x801271f8 x5. If actor+0x580 can ever reach 4+ for this family the dispatch would jump to NULL; a port that mirrors family 1's 9-slot default-stub layout for family 2 would be wrong. LIKELY the +0x4ec command records for family 2 never emit actionIndex > 3, but that was not verified.

The two-stage wrapper split is load-bearing for re-entry semantics: the engine zz_0177a3c_ runs EVERY frame of the action (it is the actionTable[3] driver), so the charge-meter halving in stage 1 (FUN_8012750c/FUN_8015bc50) also runs every frame, not once at move start — +0x18e0/+0x18e2 (resp. +0x1922/+0x1926) decay geometrically toward 0 across the whole 4-phase move. The dig's port-shape note 'on entry ... halve the two family charge meters' could be misread as halve-once; the ROM halves per dispatch tick. (Derived: the root driver dispatches (&table)[+0x580] unconditionally each frame and stage 1 is the table entry itself.)

+0x1cef, per the already-ported convention in packages/combat/src/rom/actor.ts (~line 167), is a stream-op-driven contact flag (stream op 0x02 sets it), not raw physics contact — so the phase-2 'hit' transition timing is authored in the group-2 stream slot 1 data, which lives in runtime pl-file streams. Port timing fidelity therefore depends on the stream data for group 2 slot 1, same caveat class as the animBankGravity fallback the dig already flags.

FUN_8012756c returns a uint (spawn-success bool propagated from zz_0182fcc_) and FUN_8015bcb0 returns void; the engine ignores the bctrl return in both cases (r3 unused after 0x80177cdc), so the TS onHit signature returning void is safe.

zz_0182fcc_ and zz_0084600_ additionally set child byte +0x00 = 1 (pool-active flag) before the kind/callback writes — present in both spawner decompiles, omitted from the dig's spawner summaries; irrelevant if the port reuses the existing spawner pipeline, but load-bearing for any fresh child-pool implementation.

claims.statement: SharedXConfig-analog for this machine (mirror of shared-x-special.ts, distinct engine — do NOT merge with zz_00ff2bc_'s port): interface SharedXBlinkStrikeConfig { groundSlot: number /* cfg u16+0x00: 0 (fam1) / 1 (fam2,3) */; airSlot: number /* cfg u16+0x02: 1 / 2 / 2 */; repositionScale: number /* cfg f32+0x04 = 0.95 all three */; onSetup: ((actor)=>void)|null /* cfg+0x0c; only fam3: yVel=4.0, controlWord|=0x40 */; onSteer: ((actor)=>void)|null /* cfg+0x10; called once in P0 then EVERY P1 tick; fam1 = face(0xc1)+pitch-seek ch+0x18e0; fam2/3 = face(0xc1)+air pitch-seek ch+0x18da */; onHit: ((actor,ctx)=>void)|null /* cfg+0x14; P1 contact transition */; pitchHalveAlways: boolean /* wrapper delta: fam1 true, fam2/3 false (only when grounded) */; whiffFrames?: number /* host approximation, same rationale as shared-x-special */ }. Machine constants (all DOL-read): STREAM_RATE=-1.0 (FLOAT_8043ae04), GRAVITY=1.0 (FLOAT_8043ae1c, applied P1 AND P2), COOLDOWN=1.0 (FLOAT_8043ae1c, +0x694=1.0+dt), PITCH_DECAY=0.9 (FLOAT_8043ae30, P2 per tick on +0x18da), TRAIL_THRESHOLD=3.0 (FLOAT_8043ae38), LANDING_DAMP=0.1 (FLOAT_8043ae3c via zz_006ed8c_ on hSpeed/yVel). Host hooks needed beyond shared-x-special's set: (a) per-tick steer callback slot; (b) aim-pitch channel state (+0x18da/+0x18e0) with halve-on-entry + 0.9 decay; (c) reposition slide continues through P1 AND P2 and is killed by onHit's motion-zeroing (fam1/2) — model motion as persistent actor state; (d) landing damp hook; (e) descriptor default-gravity reload on stream event (+0x1d0f<0 → +0x50 = desc+0x6c); (f) onFamilyProjectile hook per spawner (fam1: zz_0084600_/zz_00c3be0_ variants; fam2: zz_0182fcc_ types 0-3; fam3: zz_01b2b64_ 8-0xb + SFX 0x105 + part-flag bytes). Key behavioral differences from zz_00ff2bc_: no +0x541 prologue, gravity in P1, cooldown 1.0 not 4.0, slot NOT incremented (+0x6ea = slot, no ++), facing entirely config-driven, exit gated on +0x1cee contact byte with the three-way zz_006a5a4_/zz_006a474_/zz_006a750_(7) fork.
evidence: all field/constant citations in MACHINE-P0/P1/P2, CONFIG-MECHANISM, CFG-FAM1/2/3, CB-FAM1/2/3, WRAPPER-PROOF claims

findings.portImplications: This is the second shared X engine (zz_0179fcc_ @0x80179fcc, 3 phases @0x8017a008/0x8017a130/0x8017a208) — same architecture as zz_00ff2bc_ but a richer config (0x18 bytes: groundSlot u16, airSlot u16, repositionScale f32=0.95, reserved f32=0.1, onSetup ptr, onSteer ptr, onHit ptr) and different dynamics: gravity 1.0 integrates in BOTH phase 1 and 2, the 0.95 reposition slide persists through all three phases until onHit zeroes motion, cooldown is 1.0+dt (not 4.0), facing/steering is entirely callback-driven, and there is an aim-pitch channel (+0x18da) halved by the wrapper on entry and decayed ×0.9 per phase-2 tick. Port it as a NEW shared module (suggested families/shared-x-blink-strike.ts) parameterized by the interface in claim PORT-SHAPE; register fam1 (pl0601/09/17/1d, x-action 2, all 5 variants identical), fam2 (pl0604/18, x-action 3), fam3 (pl0613/27, x-action 3, aerial: onSetup sets yVel=4 and the 0x40 bit so it always uses air slot 2 and exits via upper-cue 7). Family onHits share the exact backdash numbers (hSpeed 40, yVel 20, decel −1, gravity from descriptor+0x6c) for fams 1/2; fam3 spawns without any backdash. Surface each family's spawners through the existing StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile hook (spawner addresses/types in claim SPAWNERS); child ballistics remain host residuals exactly like the ninja shuriken."
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared X-machine engine zz_0179fcc_ @0x80179fcc complete. Engine = bare 3-phase dispatcher (table PTR_FUN_80352b64 = [0x8017a008, 0x8017a130, 0x8017a208], indexed by +0x540, r4 config passed through untouched; no +0x541 prologue). Backdash trigger nuance in the fam1/fam2 onHit callbacks: the 'spawned' flag that gates the backdash velocity writes is set by control flow, not by allocation success. FUN_800cfa38 sets bVar2=true whenever the ammo gate passes, even if s16 +0x3e8 matched no case; FUN_801261a0 sets bVar6=true unconditionally in the 0x604 branch and overwrites the zz_0182fcc_ result with true in the 0x618 branch. So the backdash fires even when the projectile pool allocation fails — the port should not tie the velocity writes to spawn success.

extraFindings: CORRECTED VALUES for the port: shared anim/aux pointers stored by all three ctors are +0x1d84 = 0x802bcc64 and +0x1d8c = 0x802bb698 (the dig's 0x802ccc64/0x802cb698 dropped the borrow when adding the negative addi to lis 0x802c0000).

Aim-pitch seek semantics load-bearing for the port: in BOTH zz_006e848_ and zz_006e1d0_ the literal (0x2000 / param_4=0x4000) clamps the TARGET pitch angle, and the per-tick STEP is the descriptor rate (desc+0xae for mask 0xc1); they are not max-step limits. Additionally the two helpers scale that rate by DIFFERENT multipliers: zz_006e848_ by dt (+0x1dc8), zz_006e1d0_ by *(float*)(actor+0x768).

No-target behavior differs per channel: zz_006e1d0_ (+0x18da, fam2/3) decays the channel x0.9 (FLOAT_804376d8) when +0xcc==0; zz_006e848_ (+0x18e0, fam1) does NOTHING when +0xcc==0 (returns -1, channel frozen). A port that decays +0x18e0 on target loss would be wrong.
zz_006e848_ has a param_3 sign-flip branch (param_3!=0 negates the target); fam1 passes 0 so it is inert here but matters if the helper is shared.

claims.statement: SharedXConfig-analog for this machine (mirror of shared-x-special.ts): interface SharedMorphXConfig { groundSlot: number /* cfg s8@0, group-4 stream slot when grounded */; airSlot: number /* cfg s8@1 */; groundForm: number /* cfg s16@2, borg id morphed into when grounded */; airForm: number /* cfg s16@4 */; flightExitForm: number /* cfg s16@6; -1 = never; morph target === this -> exit dispatchUpperBodyCue(actor, 0x1b) instead of idle/air-fall */; repositionScale: number /* cfg f32@8; 0.98 for all nine ROM blocks */; preEngine?: (actor) => void /* family-4 wrapper work: borg 0x611 halves +0x1922/+0x1926; 0x622/0x625 halve +0x18e0/+0x18e2 */ }. Host hooks needed beyond the existing StreamContext: (1) morphActor(actor, borgId, playerSlot) — the zz_006a8c0_ analog: set borg number, bump form counter (+0x3f0), carry skin bytes (+0x3e4/+0x3e7), swap model/descriptor, then run the FUN_800562b8-analog weapon/ammo re-init and the zz_017a608_-analog gun-state wipe; (2) playSfx(0xb2) at phase 0; (3) clearChainCancelMasks(0xff); (4) the three existing exits. Machine skeleton: phase 0 = blink-away (motion=(pos-target)*0.98, pos+=motion), zero-nothing (unlike zz_00ff2bc_ it does NOT zero +0x44..+0x50), pick slot/form by grounded, startStream(0xf, group 4, slot, rate -1.0), remember pendingForm (+0x548); phase 1 = tickStream(0xf), motion*=0.98 continue, on stream END (the +0x1cee event — the same whiff-frames approximation gred.ts/shared-x-special.ts use applies, since stream banks are not byte-loaded) do morph + wipe + controlWord&=~3 + exit select. No cooldown seed, no timers in the shared engine. pl061a (PROTO EAGLE) must instead be registered with a bespoke 4-phase machine (see CFG-FAM-EAGLE-061A-BESPOKE) whose hooks are the existing ammo gate (slot 2, cost 1), projectile spawns types 0xc/0xd via the onFamilyProjectile hook, gravity integratePhysics(1.0), and cooldowns 16.0 (no-ammo) / 8.0 (landing) + dt.

Exact gameplay meaning of the gun-state fields +0x18da/+0x1d64/+0x1d68/+0x1922/+0x1926/+0x18e0/+0x18e2 (heat vs spread vs barrel-spin).
findings.portImplications: This engine is NOT another SharedXConfig clone — it is the Machine-Borg TRANSFORM X-special. Port it as a second shared machine (e.g. families/shared-morph-x.ts) with SharedMorphXConfig {groundSlot, airSlot, groundForm, airForm, flightExitForm, repositionScale, preEngine?}. Machine shape: 2 phases only. P0: blink-away reposition (motion=(pos−lockTarget)×0.98, pos+=motion — NOTE scale comes from config, and unlike zz_00ff2bc_ it does NOT zero +0x44..+0x50), SFX 0xb2, clear chain-cancel masks, pick {slot, pendingForm} by grounded, startStream(mask 0xf, group 4, slot, rate −1.0). P1: tickStream(0xf), reposition continue ×0.98, per-tick gun-state decay (+0x18da>>=1, +0x1d64/+0x1d68 ×0.9 truncated), and on stream END (+0x1cee — use the same whiff-frames stand-in as shared-x-special.ts since stream banks aren't byte-loaded) do: morphActor(pendingForm) [swap borg id, bump form counter, carry skin bytes, weapon/ammo re-init per FUN_800562b8, zero the 15 gun-state fields per zz_017a608_], controlWord&=~3, then exit = (pendingForm===flightExitForm) ? dispatchUpperBodyCue(0x1b) : grounded ? groundIdleReset : airFall. No cooldown/timers in the shared engine. Registrations: TITAN pl0604/pl0618→{0,0,0x605,0x605,−1}; EAGLE pl0606→{0,0,0x607,0x607,0x607}; VICTORY KING 0x610→{0,1,0x611,0x612,0x612}; 0x611→pre-halve(+0x1922/+0x1926)+{0,1,0x610,0x612,0x612}; DUKE 0x621↔0x622 {0,0,…,−1}; BARON 0x623→{1,1,0x624,0x624,0x624}; VICTORY MACHINE 0x625→pre-halve(+0x18e0/+0x18e2)+{1,1,0x626,0x626,0x626}; PANTHER pl0613/pl0627→{0,0,0x614,0x614,−1}. PROTO EAGLE pl061a needs a separate bespoke 4-phase gun machine (constants: reposition 0.95, gravity 1.0, cooldown 16.0 no-ammo / 8.0 landing + dt, heat decay 0.9, ammo slot 2 cost 1, projectile types 0xc/0xd via onFamilyProjectile). Big blocker to flag: the morph targets are borg ids with no borgs.json entries (vehicle forms) — the host must be able to instantiate/re-skin those alt-form actors (and their families own the morph-back X) before this port is user-visible; until then the honest option is registering the machine with a no-op morph hook.
findings.summary: Engine 0x8017a374 decoded transcription-grade: it is the Machine-Borg line's shared TRANSFORM (morph) X-special, a 2-phase machine (table 0x804347b0 = [0x8017a3ac, 0x8017a4a8]) whose r4 config (12 bytes, stride 0xc: s8 groundSlot, s8 airSlot, s16 groundForm, s16 airForm, s16 flightExitForm, f32 repositionScale=0.98) selects a group-4 stream slot and a target borg id; phase 1 decays gun-state fields, and on stream end (+0x1cee) swaps the actor's borg number via zz_006a8c0_ (Ghidra dropped its 3rd arg r5=+0x3ec), wipes 15 gun-state fields + re-inits weapon/ammo (zz_017a608_/FUN_800562b8), and exits via upper cue 0x1b (when morphing to the flight form), ground idle, or air-fall. All nine per-borg config blocks dumped and decoded (TITAN 0x604/0x618→0x605; EAGLE 0x606→0x607; VICTORY KING 0x610↔0x611 with shared air form 0x612; DUKE 0x621↔0x622; BARON 0x623→0x624; VICTORY MACHINE 0x625→0x626; PANTHER 0x613/0x627→0x614), wrapper→engine bl proven by raw disasm for every family, family-4 wrappers pre-halve heat fields, and PROTO EAGLE pl061a was found to bypass the engine entirely with a bespoke 4-phase gun machine (phase table 0x803319ec, projectiles 0xc/0xd, cooldowns 16/8) — fully transcribed. PROTO KING/VICTORY TANK have dead X branches (UNRESOLVED why).

The morph identity is byte-derived: zz_006a8c0_ writes actor+0x3e8 (verified sth r4,0x3e8) fired on the +0x1cee test in phase 1. Gun-state decay/wipe ops verified in P1/wipe claims.

Prescriptive claim, but every ROM fact it rests on re-verified: config field mapping (offsets 0/1/2/4/6/8, 0.98 everywhere), the negative finding that P0 does NOT zero +0x44..+0x50 (no such stores in 0x8017a3ac-0x8017a4a4, unlike the zz_00ff2bc_ machine per nn-family-decode), no cooldown/timer writes in the shared engine, family-4 pre-halve must precede the machine (halving is before the bl), pl061a bespoke needs (ammo gate (2,1,1), spawns 0xc/0xd, gravity 1.0, cooldowns 16.0/8.0+dt). Mirrored structure shared-x-special.ts:26-48 (SHARED_X consts + SharedXConfig interface) confirmed. One simplification available: morphActor need not take playerSlot — the ROM helper re-reads +0x3ec from the actor itself (see extraFindings).

Every listed instruction re-disassembled this session and matches exactly: TITAN 0x801260d0/0x801260d8/0x801260dc; EAGLE 0x8012a4ec/0x8012a4f4/0x8012a4f8; pl061a 0x8012a51c addi 0x19ec + bctrl dispatch, no engine bl; VICTORY 0x8015ad64/0x8015ad6c/0x8015ad70, 0x8015ad94/0x8015ad98, 0x8015adbc/0x8015adc0; VEHICLE 0x8015bba0/0x8015bbbc, 0x8015bbe0/0x8015bbfc, 0x8015bc20/0x8015bc3c; PANTHER 0x8018cb28/0x8018cb30/0x8018cb34. Handler heads: 0x801260b4 bl, 0x8012a4bc lha + cmpwi 0x606 (cmpwi is at 0x8012a4c0, one insn after the lha — trivially imprecise phrasing, facts right), 0x8015ad1c lha + cmpwi chain, 0x8015bb50 lha + cmpwi chain, 0x8018cb0c bl. All bl targets resolve to 0x8017a374 <zz_017a374_>.

Verified as the LIKELY-labeled claim it is: the ops themselves are byte-derived (decay disasm 0x8017a4d8-0x8017a538, wipe list 8017a608_zz_017a608.c:6-20, wrapper halving 0x8015bba4-0x8015bbb8 all re-checked); actor.ts:61-63 does document +0x18da as 'phase-2 steering yaw' and the gcrash doc (~lines 107-123) shows +0x18da driving sin/cos XZ velocity and being ticked in phase 3 — so the 'family-specific reuse of the same scratch block' caveat is warranted. Appropriately labeled LIKELY.

Byte facts re-derived: lha r3,0x548(r30) @0x8017a5b0, lha r0,6(r31) @0x8017a5b4, cmpw/bne, li r4,0x1b @0x8017a5c4, bl zz_006a750_ @0x8017a5c8. zz_006a750_ decomp confirmed as the cue-table row consumer (cueTable[cue*2+1], -1 = no-op returning 0). The flight/vehicle-pose semantic of cue 27 is correctly left unverified (LIKELY) — the families' cue tables were not dumped. Verdict is on the claim as stated, which is properly hedged.

Raw disasm re-derived: FUN_8015ad10 cmpwi 0x610/0x621/0x623 and FUN_8015bb44 cmpwi 0x611/0x622/0x625, both with bne-to-next chains ending at the epilogue — no default case, no +0x540 write for 0x61e/0x61f. Decomp files match. The upstream command-table/descriptor question is correctly flagged unchecked (UNRESOLVED label appropriate).

extraFindings: DEAD-ARGUMENT NUANCE (affects MACHINE-P1/MACHINE-TRANSFORM-HELPER/PORT-SHAPE): the engine loads r5 = lbz 0x3ec(r30) @0x8017a58c before bl zz_006a8c0_, but zz_006a8c0_ never reads incoming r5 — it clobbers r5 with its own lbz r5,0x3ec(r31) @0x8006a958 for the zz_01cb750_ event post. Ghidra's 2-param signature for zz_006a8c0_ is therefore correct and the engine's r5 load is dead code. Consequence for the port: morphActor(actor, borgId) does not need a playerSlot parameter; the slot byte should be read from the actor inside the morph helper, matching the ROM.

+0x1cee NAMING CONFLICT: packages/combat/src/rom/actor.ts:169 documents +0x1cee as 'wallContact: wall-contact flag (G Crash phase 3 polls for ground/wall to end the move)', while packages/combat/src/families/shared-melee-lunge.ts (comment near line 161) calls the same byte 'Stream end (+0x1cee)'. The dig's evidence cite 'actor.ts:169 — +0x1cee previously established as the stream-end/part event byte' is therefore only half-supported; only the shared-melee-lunge comment supports the stream-end reading. The engine's test of +0x1cee is byte-verified regardless; the port should reconcile the two interpretations (both may be the same underlying stream/part event channel) rather than inherit two names for one byte.

INDEPENDENT CTOR-TO-HANDLER CONFIRMATION (strengthens all CFG-FAM claims): a DOL-wide pointer scan finds each X handler address stored exactly once, inside the copy-source table of its claimed family ctor: FUN_801260a8 @0x8032f424 (ctor 0x80125a28 copies from 0x8032f3c8, delta +0x5c), FUN_8012a4b0 @0x80331944 (ctor 0x80129608, table 0x803318e8, +0x5c), FUN_8015ad10 @0x803447fc (ctor 0x8015a494, table 0x80344798, +0x64), FUN_8015bb44 @0x80345bf0 (ctor 0x8015b420, table 0x80345b78, +0x78), FUN_8018cb00 @0x80365474 (ctor 0x8018c2b0, table 0x80365410, +0x64). All five ctors are the same 16-iteration table-copy loop into actor +0x4b4.. (raw disasm re-derived). The pl-id-to-ctor pairing itself still rests on the earlier fleet-scout mapping (statically consistent: each table sits immediately before its family's engine config blocks).

GHIDRA ARG-DROPPING IS DISPATCHER/WRAPPER-ONLY: the phase-function decomps FUN_8017a3ac/FUN_8017a4a8 in organized/game/combat/knockback DO retain the config as param_2 (e.g. '*(float *)(param_2 + 8)'), so the corpus text for the phases is directly usable; only zz_017a374_ and the leaf wrappers lost arguments. Each phase file exists under the claimed knockback bucket paths and matches the raw disasm 1:1.

MINOR: pl0625 VICTORY MACHINE is tribe 'Tank Borg' in borgs.json (like pl061f), not Machine Borg — the SEMANTIC-IDENTITY phrase 'Machine Borg line' is loose for the vehicle-form family, though the transform behavior claims are unaffected.

MINOR ADDRESS IMPRECISION in CFG-FAM-EAGLE-0606: the claim says handler '@0x8012a4bc does lha...cmpwi 0x606' — the lha is at 0x8012a4bc and the cmpwi at 0x8012a4c0. Facts correct, address covers only the lha.

ADDITIONAL DETAIL not in the claims: the ctor copy-tables place the X handler at different slot offsets per family (+0x5c vs +0x64 vs +0x78), meaning the action-handler slot layout is not uniform across families — worth knowing before assuming a fixed 'action 2' column when porting the remaining families.

extraFindings: DEAD ARGUMENT (port-relevant simplification): zz_006a8c0_ never reads its incoming r5 — at 0x8006a958 it reloads lbz r5,0x3ec(r31) from the actor before the zz_01cb750_ event post. The engine's lbz r5,0x3ec(r30) @0x8017a58c is therefore dead code at the call boundary (value identical since the engine passes the same actor's field). A ported morphActor(actor, borgId) needs no third playerSlot parameter; the event-post analog should read the actor's own +0x3ec.

FUN_800562b8 does MORE re-init than the MACHINE-HELPER-WIPE claim enumerates; a faithful morph re-init also needs: +0x589 <- descr+0xb6; floats +0x5f4/+0x768/+0x664 <- FLOAT_80437258; +0x8cc <- descr+0x182; +0x204/+0x208 <- descr+0x98; the 3-iteration loop setting +0x63c/+0x648 floats (FLOAT_80437250) and calling FUN_8013bd80(actor, i) for each command byte cmd+0xbb+i != -1; +0x655 = 0; and the +0x7cd charge flag derived from cmd+0x11 and +0x661 (decomp lines 41-59, 99-121 of 800562b8_knockback-targeting_candidate_800562b8.c).

Handler-table corroboration independent of ctor disasm: each family X handler pointer was found in the DOL data section inside its family's function-pointer table — TITAN 0x8032f424, EAGLE 0x80331944, VICTORY 0x803447fc, VEHICLE 0x80345bf0, PANTHER 0x80365474 — all at the same relative slot (base-8 word dumps show handlers uniformly at index 2, i.e. action 2 = X), matching the pl0604/pl0618, pl0606/pl061a, pl0610/.../pl0623, pl061f/pl0625, pl0613/pl0627 memberships in actionStreamTables.json (constructorAddress fields for 0x80125a28/0x80129608/0x8015a494/0x8015b420/0x8018c2b0).

The VEHICLE family table 0x80345bf0 has NULL words at +0x8/+0xc (slots 4/5) where every other family has function pointers — the vehicle forms support fewer actions; worth checking when porting the 0x611/0x622 runtime forms' full action set.

r2 = 0x8043ea20 confirmed by fingerprint: lfd -0x3c18(r2) resolves to 0x8043ae08 which contains exactly 0x43300000_80000000 — the mandatory s16-to-double conversion magic constant — proving the r2 base value used throughout the dig's -0x3xxx(r2) resolutions.

0x611 is absent from borgs.json just like 0x605/0x607/0x612/0x614/0x622/0x624/0x626 (SEMANTIC-IDENTITY's absence list omitted it); all nine alt-form ids referenced by the config blocks are non-selectable.

zz_006a750_ returns 0 (no state change) when cueTable[0x1b*2+1] == -1: families whose cue table lacks a cue-27 row would silently fall through the flightExit path with NO exit dispatched at all (the engine does not fall back to idle/air-fall in that case — the bne skips them). Cue-table rows for cue 27 in these five families should be dumped before porting the flightExit path.

claims.statement: PORT SHAPE (mirror of shared-x-special.ts, new shared layer 'satellite-x-special'): export const SAT_X = { WINDUP_TIMER: 0.0 /*FLOAT_8043afac*/, RECOVERY: 30.0 /*FLOAT_8043afb0*/, AMMO_SLOT: 2, AMMO_COST: 1, EXIT_CUE: 0x1b, LAUNCH_TILT_Y: -0.1 /*FLOAT_80439230*/ }. interface SatelliteXConfig { satelliteVariant: 0|1 /* the zz_00fb220_ r4 arg; 0 iff borg 0x0d01 */; onDeploySatellite: (actor, variant) => void /* host hook = zz_00fb220_: spawn type-0x4d child with SAT_RECORD */; } where SAT_RECORD (shared by both variants) = { muzzleBone: 1, muzzleOffset: [0,-80,-10], launchSpeed: 10.0, vy0: -5.0, vyAccel: -0.1, homingRate: 3.0, homingInterval: 4, lifeFrames: 600, shotBudget: 10, hoverArg: 200.0 }. Machine: P0 phase=1, timer=0; P1 phase=2, timer=30, if ammoGate(slot2,cost1) { deployedLatch=true; onDeploySatellite(cfg.satelliteVariant); } (NO whiffFrames needed — no stream dependency); P2 timer-=dt, on <=0 → clear ctrl bits (+0x5e0&=~3), dispatch full-body cue 0x1b (idle state 0), NO cooldown write. Host must also clear deployedLatch (+0x145) when ammo slot 2 refills (family update hook), and both families register with cueTable 0x803627b8 rows and X→action1 for command types 2 AND 5.

findings.portImplications: New shared machine, NOT a SharedXConfig reuse: unlike zz_00ff2bc_ (config-block-in-r4 pattern), the pl0d pair binds one engine (0x80188da4) through one shared action table (0x80362658) installed by both ctors, and forks per-family INSIDE phase 1 on borg id (0x0d01→satellite variant 0, else 1). Port as packages/combat/src/families/satellite-x-special.ts exporting createSatelliteXSpecial(cfg) with cfg = { satelliteVariant: 0|1, onDeploySatellite(actor, variant) } and constants { windup 0.0, recovery 30.0, ammo slot 2 cost 1, exit cue 0x1b, no cooldown seed } — wire as actionTable[1] for BOTH pl0d01 and pl0d05; command types 2 AND 5 both route to it (cue 0x2d → full-body 61). Registration needs the shared cue table 0x803627b8 (family-0-like rows, sparse: 0,9-11,27,28,35,36,43-47). The X payload is a spawned homing satellite child (type 0x4d): both variants share one record — muzzle bone 1 offset (0,-80,-10), launch speed 10 with dir.y pre-tilt -0.1, then vy=-5.0 with accel -0.1/frame (this IS the 'gravity -0.1' satellite drift — it lives in the CHILD record, not actor physics; the actor never moves during the special), homing re-steer every 4 frames at rate 3.0 toward lockTarget pos, life 600 frames, 10-shot budget, despawn on owner death/borg-swap. Host hooks needed: (1) StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile-style satellite spawn hook, (2) a family-update hook clearing the deployed latch (+0x145) when ammo slot 2 refills (max @+0x78e+slot*8, cur @+0x774+slot*8) — re-deploy gating is ammo-regen-driven, not timer-driven, (3) button-held bit (+0x5bc & 0x200) only needed if the B actions (turret burst action 0 with 0.2-rate pitch-only aim clamp [0,0x4000] and 10-round hold-fire; alternating pod launcher action 2 recs 0x10/0x11 muzzles (-15.48,0,41.44)/(34.43,0,57.15) speed 15) are ported in the same pass. No whiffFrames approximation is required anywhere — the machine is purely timer-driven (30.0 recovery), the first ported family machine with zero stream-VM dependency.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig complete for engine 0x80188da4 (pl0d01/pl0d05 satellite pair, x-action 1). Architecture finding: 0x80362674 is the shared PHASE TABLE [0x80188de0, 0x80188df8, 0x80188e7c] and there are NO per-family wrappers at all — both Ghidra-missed ctors (0x8018882c/0x8018890c, raw-disassembled) copy 16-slot blocks (0x803625d8/0x80362618) whose +0x4b4 is the same root dispatcher FUN_80188b24 → shared action table 0x80362658 with entry[1] = the engine; per-family variation is a runtime borg-id switch (s16 +0x3e8 == 0x0d01) inside phase 1 selecting satellite variant 0/1. The X machine is purely timer-driven (windup 0.0, recovery 30.0 = FLOAT_8043afb0, dt at +0x1dc8): no streams, no contact flags, no actor velocity/gravity writes, exit via zz_006a53c_(actor,0) → full-body cue 0x1b → idle state 0 with NO +0x694 cooldown. Payload: ammo-slot-2-gated (zz_006dbe0_(actor,2,1,1), latch +0x145 cleared when slot-2 ammo refills in family update FUN_80188a64) spawn of homing-satellite child type 0x4d via zz_00fb220_ — child records dumped (life 600, fire interval 4, speed 10, vy -5, y-accel -0.1 = the satellite drift, homing rate 3.0, muzzle bone 1 @(0,-80,-10); variants byte-identical except one setup s16). Command tables both route types 2 AND 5 → cue 0x2d/action 1; B commands diverge (pl0d01→action 2 pod launcher type 0x4c; pl0d05→action 0 pitch-clamped turret burst via zz_0082824_ recs 0x10/0x11, dumped). Sibling actions 0/2 fully transcribed, cue table 0x803627b8 dumped, wrapper-proof chains established for both families. 20 claims: 17 DERIVED, 2 LIKELY, 1 UNRESOLVED.

extraFindings: MACH-7 nuance (fire cadence): FUN_80188c54 fires when u16 +0x14c == 0 and then sets it to 10, decrementing on non-fire frames — the actual firing period is 11 frames, not 10. The reload constant 10 in the claim is correct; a port should reproduce the check-then-reload-10 structure rather than hardcode 'every 10 frames'.

MACH-7 nuance (shot counting): in FUN_80188c54 the shot counter +0x14e increments BEFORE the zz_006dbe0_ ammo gate, so denied-ammo ticks still count toward the 1-shot/10-shot exit conditions — the burst can end having fired fewer projectiles than counted.

MACH-7 nuance (no lock target): FUN_8006cc90's +0xcc==0 branch (no lock target) returns 1 (same as converged) after decaying the aim shorts, so action-0 phase 1 advances to the firing phase on its first frame when unlocked; the claim's 'converged OR timer<=0' wording hides this instant-advance path (organized/game/combat/knockback/8006cc90_knockback-targeting_candidate_8006cc90.c:47-59 sets uVar5=1 in that branch).

PF-4 omission (flight-phase impact exits): FUN_800fb56c has two additional despawn paths not listed in the claim — zz_0083714_(child)!=0 → zz_00fb76c_ despawn with no explosion, and (when zz_0083714_==0) zz_0084170_(child)!=0 → zz_00fb76c_ + zz_0085e00_(child,pos,7) explosion. These look like the hit/impact exits and matter for satellite lifetime fidelity. The flight phase also calls zz_00833ec_(child) and zz_0083610_(child,&vy) each frame (orientation/attitude helpers) and decrements lifetime +0x1c by 1 per frame.
PF-3 nuance: FUN_800fb330's owner-gone/borg-changed force-despawn guard only runs while child phase +0x18 < 2 (deploy/fly); once despawned (phase 2/3) the guard is skipped.

PF-6 nuance: zz_00c74ec_ passes *(int*)(actor+0x90) (the slot object) as the allocator owner arg — not the actor — and is globally gated on *PTR_DAT_80433930 != 5; on success it also ORs bit (1 << child[0x12]) into slot+0x708 when actor+0x83 is 0 or 0x18. None of this changes the claim's conclusion.

CFG-2 observation: both ctors open with a dead guard 'li r0,0; cmpwi r0,0x10; bge <skip>' — an unrolled-loop artifact; the 16-word copy always executes. Purely cosmetic, but explains the odd shape if anyone re-reads the disasm.
CFG-2 caveat: the anim-bank table 0x803c4d94 is BSS (addr_to_off returns None), so its contents cannot be dol-read; the +0x4ac binding claim rests correctly on the instruction pattern only.

MACH-7 port nuance: in FUN_80188c54 (action-0 P2) the shot counter +0x14e increments on every 10-frame firing tick BEFORE the ammo gate, so dry-fires (ammo denied) still count toward the 1-shot tap-exit and 10-shot held-exit conditions. A port that only counts successful shots would diverge when slot-0 ammo runs out mid-burst.

MACH-7 port nuance: FUN_8006cc90 returns 1 immediately when there is no lock target (+0xcc==0, the decay branch), so action-0 P1 advances to the firing phase on the first frame when unlocked; the 10.0-frame timer only matters while a lock target exists and the aim is still converging.

PF-3 omitted guard: the force-despawn check in FUN_800fb330 (and FUN_800fce50) is additionally gated on child phase +0x18 < 2 — a child already in despawn phase 2/3 is not re-released; a port calling the release helper every frame would double-release.

All three action dispatchers (0x80188b24 root, 0x80188b60, 0x80188da4, 0x80188ec4) sign-extend the index byte (extsb) before scaling — negative +0x580/+0x540 values would index BEFORE the table base. Not reachable in normal flow but worth preserving byte-typed (s8) semantics in the port.

Family config instance: { groundSlot: 0, airSlot: 1, onHit: deathBorgOnHit, whiffFrames: TUNED }. deathBorgOnHit(actor, ctx): (a) pop — actor.lockYaw(+0x5ae) = heading(+0x72) − 0x8000; hSpeed(+0x44)=10.0; yVel(+0x48)=15.0; hDecel(+0x4c)=0.0; gravityCoeff(+0x50)=−1.0 (ROM gates the pop on stream-event +0x1d0f; ninja.ts precedent applies for how the port models that gate); (b) ammo gate slot 2 cost 1 (host hook, same as ninja.ts); on success, ONLY for borgId 'pl000c', surface the projectile via the existing StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile hook with (table 0x802d6d68, variant 5) — bone 11, muzzle (55,0,0), texId 2, kind 1, no bank flags, no +0x7b0 offset.
