findings.portImplications: Zero new engine work: this family is a pure SharedXConfig instantiation of the already-ported shared-x-special.ts machine. Concretely: (1) new families/death-borg.ts (or extend a shared registry) with cfg { groundSlot: 0, airSlot: 1, onHit } — slots identical to ninja so stream wiring is already exercised; (2) onHit = ninja-backflip-shaped pop with the SAME four constants (10.0 / 15.0 / 0.0 / −1.0, yaw −0x8000) — can literally share ninja.ts's pop helper, only the projectile branch differs; (3) projectile: ammo slot 2 cost 1, then pl000c-ONLY spawn of table 0x802d6d68 variant 5 through the existing StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile hook (variant 5 is already present in shotVariantKinds.json as kind 1 / borg 0xc, so the host-side flight-visual decode needs no new data); pl0008 spawns nothing (still calls the ammo gate in ROM). (4) CRITICAL gating: pl0008's X-special is COMMAND-DISABLED at the descriptor (mode byte +0xb8[2] = 0xff vs pl000c's 0x01) — register the X action only for pl000c, or replicate the initiator's mode-byte rejection; wiring shared-X for pl0008 would add a move the real game never allows. whiffFrames stays a TUNED port-side value (default 60) as in gred.ts/ninja.ts. Cue-table/bridge work not covered here (ctor binds +0x27c = 0x80376960, LIKELY this family's 48x2 cue table — separate dump needed before bridging, same shape as family-0 @0x802d3ff8).
findings.summary: Confirmed end-to-end: Death Borg Alpha family (pl0008 DEATH BORG ALPHA / pl000c DEATH BORG ALPHA II, ctor zz_019e9a4_ @0x8019e9a4) routes X-action 2 through action table @0x8037643c[2] = 0x801a0414, whose inner wrapper 0x801a0434 loads r4 = r13−0x5e50 = 0x80435750 and bl's the already-ported shared X machine zz_00ff2bc_ @0x800ff2bc (raw-disasm proven; Ghidra dropped the r4 arg). Config @0x80435750 = {groundSlot 0, airSlot 1, onHit 0x801a0458} — same slots as ninja. The onHit callback zz_01a0458_ is a ninja-shaped backflip pop (yaw −0x8000; +0x44=10.0, +0x48=15.0, +0x4c=0.0, +0x50=−1.0 — byte-identical values from a different literal pool: FLOAT_8043b51c/8043b538/8043b4d0/8043b4cc) plus ammo gate zz_006dbe0_(actor,2,1,1) and, ONLY for borg 0xc (pl000c), shot spawn zz_007db5c_(actor,5) → table 0x802d6d68 record 5 @0x802d6e80 (bone 11, muzzle (55,0,0), texId 2, kind 1, borg-gate 0xc, no bank flags — identical ballistics to ninja's record 0). Key borg switch beyond the callback: pl0008's descriptor (0x803765e0) mode bytes `01 00 ff ff ff ff` DISABLE the X-special entirely; only pl000c (descriptor 0x803766f8, `01 00 01 ff ff ff`) can fire it. Port is a pure SharedXConfig instantiation — no engine changes.

verdicts.note: All cited lines match: ctor file lines 6/23/29-31 exact; dol read_u32(0x803763e8)=0x8019edbc; borgs.json:156-157 pl0008 DEATH BORG ALPHA, :232-233 pl000c DEATH BORG ALPHA II, both tribe Death Borg; disasm 0x801a04c4 lha r0,0x3e8(r31); cmpwi r0,0xc confirmed. STRONGER independent proof found: family-ctor pointer table @0x802d2e98 indexed by borg number has [0x8]=[0xc]=0x8019e9a4 and [0x0]=[0xa]=0x8006f4f8 (the verified ninja ctor), pinning the borg coverage directly rather than by inference. Ghidra '1000' = 0x3e8 correct.

8019edbc_zz_019edbc.c:6 matches exactly (s8 index at +0x580). My DOL dump @0x8037643c reproduces all 8 entries verbatim: 0x8019edf8/0x8019f8ec/0x801a0414/0x8019ee34/0x8019f0a8/0x8019f0a8/0x8019f314/0x8019f314. The '[2] = X-special' label is additionally proven by the command type-2 record table @0x803768e8 (root 0x80376948 via ptr @0x80376420, ctor line 20): all 24 records = [cue 44/45, ctrl 3, actionIndex 2, variant 0-4].

Capstone disasm reproduces every quoted instruction: 0x801a0420 bl 0x801a0434; 0x801a043c addi r4,r13,-0x5e50; 0x801a0444 bl 0x800ff2bc. r13=0x8043b5a0 re-verified from raw disasm @0x80003334 (lis r13,0x8043; ori r13,0xb5a0) => r4=0x80435750. Both organized decomp files show the arg-less trampolines as claimed. No variant sub-dispatch: driver body is prologue+bl+epilogue only, and command records confirm variants 0-4 all funnel here undispatched.

800ff2bc_zz_00ff2bc.c:6-9 matches (prologue + s8 +0x540 dispatch). DOL @0x8031f000 = [0x800ff30c, 0x800ff418, 0x800ff4d4, 0x0] reproduced. Bonus disasm check: engine never writes r4 before bctrl (0x800ff2bc-0x800ff2f8), table base built as lis r5,-0x7fce; addi -0x1000 = 0x8031f000. shared-x-special.ts is exactly 175 lines; SHARED_X constants 0.95/-1.0/1.0/4.0/3.0 all re-read from DOL (804392d0/b8/d4/d8/c8) and match.

r4 liveness proven by disasm (engine touches only r0/r5/r12 before bctrl). P0 800ff30c lines 28-35 exact: cfg+0 -> +0x6ea, airborne (+0x5e0&0x40) -> cfg[1], slot post-increment then zz_004beb8_(FLOAT_804392b8, actor, 0xf, 4, (int)cVar1, -1, -1) with FLOAT_804392b8 = -1.0 (DOL-read). P1 800ff418:13-17 exact (NULL-checked ptr cfg+4 called with actor only). P2 800ff4d4 takes a single param and never references param_2 — no config use. 8-byte {u16,u16,ptr} layout follows.

My DOL read @0x80435750 = 00000001 801a0458 801a31d8 801a31d8 — groundSlot 0, airSlot 1, onHit 0x801a0458, matching ninja's slots 0/1. 0x801a0458 verified as a real function: prologue then lbz r0,0x1d0f(r3) at 0x801a046c, immediately after the wrapper's blr at 0x801a0454. Engine reads stop at cfg+7 so the trailing 801a31d8 words are correctly excluded.

Decomp 801a0458 lines 15-26 match the quote verbatim. Disasm reproduces every claimed instruction and store pairing (f3=10.0->+0x44, f2=0.0->+0x4c, f1=15.0->+0x48, f0=-1.0->+0x50); r2-relative offsets -0x3504/-0x34e8/-0x3550/-0x3554 vs r2=0x8043ea20 resolve to 0x8043b51c/38/4d0/4cc = 10.0/15.0/0.0/-1.0 (DOL-read). Ammo call at 0x801a04a8 is the beq target, i.e. outside the +0x1d0f block: li r4,2/r5,1/r6,1; bl 0x8006dbe0; cmpwi r3,0; then lha +0x3e8; cmpwi 0xc; li r4,5; bl 0x8007db5c. Function ends at 0x801a04ec blr — no other borg branches. Ninja comparison file 800715f8 confirmed (three borg branches 0/3/10; type 2 conditional on +0x7c8). Disasm detail: heading transform is addis r3,r3,1 then addi -0x8000 = +0x8000 mod 2^16, identical to the decomp's '+ -0x8000' in 16-bit BAM.

8007db5c decomp lines 10-47 match: stride 0x38, gate s16@+0x2e (DAT_802d6d96), bone byte @+0x02 * 0x30 + 0x8d4 matrix pick, muzzle ptr -0x7fd29294 = u32 0x802d6d6c = base+0x04, +0x2c flag gates the +0x7b0 PSVECAdd, alloc zz_0088aa0_(actor,3,8,0,1), child [0x10]=0xf, [0x11]=variant, +0xc=FUN_8007dcec, +0x10c=FUN_8007e698. My DOL dump of rec5 @0x802d6e80 reproduces the quoted hex byte-for-byte and all decoded values (texId 2, bone 0x0b, kind 1, muzzle 55/0/0, 40.0, 4, 500.0, 0.0, 7.5, 0.15, 0.3, +0x2c=0, +0x2e=0x000c, 2.0, 0xffffffff). Diff vs rec0 is exactly 3 bytes: +0x01 (0e->02), +0x2f (00->0c), +0x35 (01->ff). shotVariantKinds.json fireSiteResolutions[20] matches the quoted fields exactly (498 entries, index 20). Caveat correctly hedged by the claim: texId/ballistic field semantics are stride-layout knowns (read by FUN_8007dcec, unread), not spawner-proven.

DOL re-read: 0x803765e0+0xb8 = 0100ffffffff, 0x803766f8+0xb8 = 010001ffffff; full 0x118 diff = exactly the 10 listed bytes (+0xba/ca/cc/d0/d2/d6/d8/f8/f9/fa with the exact values); first 0x20 bytes identical and match the quoted hex. Mode-byte semantics verified DIRECTLY (not just via the ninja doc): chunk_0009.c zz_0069dd0_ @0x80069dd0 line 462 reads descriptor(+0x4b0)[type(+0x585)+0xb8]; ==-1 -> return 0; ==1 -> zz_0069ea4_ (2D); 0 -> zz_0069fe0_ (1D). Dead-code assertion independently proven: both borgs share command root 0x80376948; type-0 records (12) all have actionIndex 0, type-1 records (6) all actionIndex 1, only type-2 records @0x803768e8 set actionIndex 2 — so with pl0008's type-2 byte = 0xff, action 2 is command-unreachable for pl0008. Non-0xba byte semantics correctly labeled UNRESOLVED.

SharedXConfig (shared-x-special.ts:35-48) has exactly {groundSlot, airSlot, onHit, whiffFrames?} — sufficient, no new fields needed. ninja.ts precedent verified: NINJA_X constants + ninjaXOnHit with ctx.onFamilyProjectile (line 71) and createSharedXSpecial wired into the actionTable (lines 383-418), per-borg setup at 423-429. All numeric constants in the proposed deathBorgOnHit trace to the confirmed callback/record claims. The recommendation to gate pl0008's X at the command layer matches the now-proven initiator behavior. One line-cite nit: registration actually lives at ninja.ts:383-429, not 34-66 (34-66 is constants + callback) — immaterial.

extraFindings: FAMILY CTOR TABLE FOUND (load-bearing for the whole fleet dig): 0x802d2e98 is a pointer table of family ctors indexed by borg number (+0x3e8). Dump [0x0..0xf]: 0x8006f4f8(ninja), 0x800ea500, 0x800cfe9c, 0x8006f60c, 0x80162128, 0x801456d4, 0x800b2ef8, 0x800a6f4c, 0x8019e9a4(death borg), 0x801456d4, 0x8006f4f8, 0x800cfe9c, 0x8019e9a4, 0x80072048, 0x80165f64, 0x80134d68. This lets every remaining family's borg coverage be pinned by table lookup instead of inference. Note DOUBLE NINJA (borg 3) has its OWN ctor 0x8006f60c, distinct from pl0000/pl000a's 0x8006f4f8.

PORT CAVEAT: packages/combat/src/data/commandMoveTables.json's entry for constructorAddress 0x8019e9a4 was extracted against pl0008's descriptor (commandStructAddress 0x803765e0), so it marks command type 2 as mode 'disabled'. Any port keying X availability off that JSON alone would wrongly disable the X-special for pl000c too — the pl000c descriptor 0x803766f8 (type-2 mode 01) is not represented there. Gate per-borg, as the dig's port-shape claim recommends.

X-special situation variants exist but are dispatch-dead: the type-2 command records @0x803768e8 carry variants 0-4 (by situation/direction, cue 44 or 45), but the action-2 driver 0x801a0414 never reads the variant byte (+0x581) — all five variants funnel to the identical shared machine + config. The port needs no per-variant handling; only the cue difference (44 vs 45 for situations 3/4) is host-visible.

Initiator zz_0069dd0_ (chunk_0009.c:456-480) also shows the chain-cancel path: when +0x5e0&3 is set, the command only fires if bit (1<<type) of +0x57a is set, then zz_005c080_ + sets +0x595=1 — relevant when wiring X-cancel windows for this family (same machinery the ninja port uses).

Engine r4-liveness proven at the instruction level: zz_00ff2bc_ @0x800ff2bc clobbers only r0/r5/r12 before bctrl (phase table base materialized as lis r5,-0x7fce; addi r5,-0x1000 = 0x8031f000), so the family config pointer arrives in the phase handlers untouched — the Ghidra arg-dropping is purely a decompiler artifact, in the engine as well as the wrappers.

verdicts.note: Independently re-derived. dol.py read_u32(0x803763e8)=0x8019edbc; ctor decomp (organized/game/physics/movement/8019e9a4_...c:6,23,29-31) sets +0x4b4 from PTR@0x803763e8, +0x4b0=&DAT_803765e0, and swaps to &DAT_803766f8 when s16 +0x3e8 (Ghidra '1000') == 0xc. borgs.json:156-157 pl0008=DEATH BORG ALPHA (tribe 'Death Borg'), :232-233 pl000c=DEATH BORG ALPHA II. STRENGTHENED beyond the claim's evidence: the per-borg ctor pointer table (index 0 at 0x802d2e98 = ninja ctor 0x8006f4f8 for pl0000) has entries [8]@0x802d2eb8 and [0xc]@0x802d2ec8 both = 0x8019e9a4, and entry [0xa] = the ninja ctor (SASUKE, matching ninja.ts) — direct proof the ctor is registered for exactly pl0008 and pl000c.

Decomp organized/game/modes/8019edbc_zz_019edbc.c:6 dispatches through &PTR_zz_019edf8__8037643c indexed by s8 actor+0x580 exactly as quoted. My dol.py dump @0x8037643c: [0]=0x8019edf8 [1]=0x8019f8ec [2]=0x801a0414 [3]=0x8019ee34 [4]=[5]=0x8019f0a8 [6]=[7]=0x8019f314 — byte-identical to the claim. Entry [2]=0x801a0414 is the X driver. The claim's own bounds caveat for entries past [2] is appropriately hedged, and the 'only types 0/1/2 command-enabled' rider is consistent with the verified +0xb8 mode bytes (pl000c: 01 00 01 ff ff ff).

My capstone disasm: 0x801a0420 `48000015 bl 0x801a0434` (driver body is prologue+bl+epilogue only, no variant sub-dispatch); wrapper 0x801a0434..0x801a0454 is exactly 9 instructions incl. 0x801a043c `388da1b0 addi r4, r13, -0x5e50` and 0x801a0444 `4bf5ee79 bl 0x800ff2bc`. r13 re-derived: __init_registers @0x80003334 = `lis r13,-0x7fbd; ori r13,r13,0xb5a0` → 0x8043b5a0, so r4 = 0x80435750. Ghidra arg-drop confirmed: organized/game/ui/801a0414_zz_01a0414.c:6 and 801a0434_zz_01a0434.c:6 both show arg-less calls.

organized/game/unsorted/800ff2bc_zz_00ff2bc.c:6-9 matches the quoted prologue + dispatch through PTR_zz_00ff30c__8031f000 indexed by s8 +0x540. Disasm confirms the table address materialization (lis r5,-0x7fce; addi r5,-0x1000 = 0x8031f000). My dol.py dump @0x8031f000 = [0x800ff30c, 0x800ff418, 0x800ff4d4, 0x0] — exact match. shared-x-special.ts exists (1-175) and its SHARED_X constants re-verified against DOL: FLOAT_804392d0=0.95(0.949999988), 804392b8=-1.0, 804392d4=1.0, 804392d8=4.0, 804392c8=3.0.

All engine config reads re-verified from decomp: 800ff30c_...c:28-31 (`(char)*param_2` → +0x6ea; airborne `+0x5e0 & 0x40` → `(char)param_2[1]`), :32-35 (slot++ then zz_004beb8_(rate,actor,0xf,4,slot,-1,-1) with rate=FLOAT_804392b8=-1.0); 800ff418_...c:13-17 (contact `'\0' < +0x1cef` → +0x540++ then NULL-checked call of *(param_2+4) with actor only); 800ff4d4 full decomp uses only param_1 — no config. Layout {u16 ground, u16 air, ptr onHit} = 8 bytes holds. One wording nit (not load-bearing): the engine dispatch is `bctrl` (call, not a tail branch) at 0x800ff2f8, but r4 is provably untouched between entry and bctrl (only r0/r5/r12 used), so the config does reach the phase handlers as param_2 exactly as claimed.

My dol.py dump @0x80435750 = `00 00 00 01 80 1a 04 58` then `80 1a 31 d8 80 1a 31 d8` — byte-identical. groundSlot=0, airSlot=1, onHit=0x801a0458. 0x801a0458 starts immediately after the wrapper (which ends at 0x801a0454) and my disasm shows a standard prologue then `lbz r0, 0x1d0f(r3)` at 0x801a046c. Slot identity with ninja re-verified: dol.py @0x80433868 = `00 00 00 01 80 07 15 f8` — same slots 0/1, only the callback pointer differs.

Full disasm 0x801a0458-0x801a04ec re-derived and matches every element: +0x1d0f gate (lbz/extsb./beq); pop block stores sth→+0x5ae, stfs f3→+0x44, f2→+0x4c, f1→+0x48, f0→+0x50; lfs displacements -0x3504/-0x3550/-0x34e8/-0x3554 vs r2=0x8043ea20 resolve to 0x8043b51c/0x8043b4d0/0x8043b538/0x8043b4cc = 10.0/0.0/15.0/-1.0 (all read_f32-verified). Ammo gate unconditional (li r4,2; li r5,1; li r6,1; bl 0x8006dbe0), then cmpwi r3,0 / lha r0,0x3e8 / cmpwi r0,0xc / li r4,5 / bl 0x8007db5c — pl0008 pays the gate check but spawns nothing; no other borg branches. Decomp 801a0458_...c:15-26 matches. Ninja contrast verified: 800715f8_...c:24-38 has exactly branches 0→type0, 3→type1(+type2 if +0x7c8!=0), 10→type3. Pedantic note: the hardware computes +0x5ae = s16(+0x72)+0x10000-0x8000 (addis 1 then addi -0x8000), i.e. +0x8000 mod 2^16 — identical to -0x8000 in 16-bit BAM, so '180° face-away' is correct.

Address math verified (0x802d6d68 + 5*0x38 = 0x802d6e80). My byte dump of record 5 is hex-identical to the claim, and every decoded field re-checked: u16+0=2, byte+2=0x0b, byte+3=1, muzzle (55.0,0,0), +0x10=40.0, +0x14=4, +0x18=500.0, +0x1c=0, +0x20=7.5, +0x24=0.15, +0x28=0.3, byte+0x2c=0, s16+0x2e=0x000c, +0x30=2.0, +0x34=0xffffffff. rec0-vs-rec5 diff is exactly 3 bytes: offset 0x01 (0x0e→0x02 texId), 0x2f (0x00→0x0c borg gate), 0x35 (0x01→0xff). Spawner decomp 8007db5c_...c:10-47 confirms stride *0x38, borg gate vs DAT_802d6d96 (base+0x2e), bone byte base+0x02 with `*0x30+0x8d4` matrix pick, muzzle ptr 0x802d6d6c (=-0x7fd29294 unsigned), +0x2c PSVECAdd gate on +0x7b0, alloc zz_0088aa0_(p,3,8,0,1), child [0x10]=0xf, [0x11]=variant, +0xc=FUN_8007dcec, +0x10c=FUN_8007e698. shotVariantKinds.json fireSiteResolutions[20] verified programmatically at exactly index 20 with the claimed fields; table rowCount=10 so record 5 is in bounds.

All four switches re-derived. (1) Ctor swap: decomp lines 29-31. (2) Mode bytes: my dumps @0x803765e0+0xb8 = `01 00 ff ff ff ff` (pl0008), @0x803766f8+0xb8 = `01 00 01 ff ff ff` (pl000c). Initiator semantics independently re-read at chunk_0009.c:462: `if (*(char *)(desc + type + 0xb8) == -1) return 0;` then 01→zz_0069ea4_ (2-D), 00→zz_0069fe0_ (1-D) at :473-479 — so X (type 2) is command-rejected for pl0008, live 2-D for pl000c. (3)+(4) callback and spawner 0xc gates verified in the disasm/decomp above. Full 0x118-byte diff independently computed: exactly 10 differing bytes at 0xba/0xca/0xcc/0xd0/0xd2/0xd6/0xd8/0xf8/0xf9/0xfa with exactly the claimed values, and the first 0x20 bytes are byte-identical matching the quoted hex prefix. The non-0xba semantics are honestly labeled UNRESOLVED; the same-shape assumption is flagged by the claimant and is consistent with the diff.

All factual anchors verified: SharedXConfig at shared-x-special.ts:35-48 has exactly {groundSlot, airSlot, onHit, whiffFrames?} — sufficient for this family (config is {0, 1, callback}, verified above); createSharedXSpecial exists (:159-175); ninja.ts provides the registration + ctx.onFamilyProjectile precedent (hook used at ninja.ts:71, actionTable wiring at :383-429 — the cited :34-66 range actually covers the constants/onHit callback, minor line-range imprecision only). All numeric constants in the proposed deathBorgOnHit match the DOL-verified values. The recommendation to gate pl0008's X at registration or the command initiator correctly mirrors the verified ROM behavior (mode byte 0xff rejected at zz_0069dd0_ before the action table is ever reached). The whiffFrames TUNED label matches the ported machine's documented approximation. This claim is part design guidance, but every checkable fact in it holds.

extraFindings: INDEPENDENT CTOR-TABLE PROOF (strengthens family-identity): the DOL contains a per-borg family-ctor pointer table with index 0 at 0x802d2e98 (= 0x8006f4f8, the ninja ctor for pl0000). Entries [8] @0x802d2eb8 and [0xc] @0x802d2ec8 both = 0x8019e9a4; entry [0xa] @0x802d2ec0 = 0x8006f4f8 (SASUKE, matching ninja.ts's 'covers pl0000 and pl000a'). This binds ctor 0x8019e9a4 to exactly pl0008 and pl000c without relying on the borg-id switch inference.

BAM arithmetic nuance in the onHit pop: the machine code is `lha r3,0x72(r31); addis r3,r3,1; addi r0,r3,-0x8000; sth r0,0x5ae(r31)` — i.e. s16(+0x72) + 0x8000 stored as u16, which is congruent mod 2^16 to the claimed '- 0x8000'. Ghidra's `+ -0x8000` print and the claim's transcription are both correct in 16-bit BAM; a porter should use (heading +/- 0x8000) & 0xffff (ninja.ts:63 already does this).

Engine dispatch wording: zz_00ff2bc_ dispatches via mtctr/bctrl at 0x800ff2f4-0x800ff2f8 (a call that returns, not a tail branch). r4 is provably untouched between function entry and the bctrl (only r0/r5/r12 are written), which is the actual reason the config pointer survives into the phase handlers. The claim's conclusion is right; 'tail-dispatch' is the only inaccuracy and it is not load-bearing.

P2 zz_00ff4d4_ fully re-read (organized/game/battle/force-setup/800ff4d4_...c): confirms zero param_2 usage AND matches the already-ported phase-2 shape in shared-x-special.ts (FUN_80067310(FLOAT_804392d4=1.0, actor, s16 +0x5ae), landing exit clearing +0x5e0 bits 0-1, zz_006a750_(actor,7)/zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_ exits, cooldown +0x694 = FLOAT_804392d8(4.0) + +0x1dc8). Its head disasm overwrites r4 with 0xf immediately (li r4,0xf @0x800ff4dc), independently confirming no config consumption.

Ninja borg-3 (DOUBLE NINJA) second shot is conditional: zz_007db5c_(p,2) fires only when byte +0x7c8 != 0 (800715f8_...c:32-34). The claim's shorthand '3→type1+2' elides this condition — irrelevant to the death-borg port but worth noting if DOUBLE NINJA is ported later.

Ninja config block re-dumped @0x80433868 = `00 00 00 01 80 07 15 f8`: same slots {0,1} as the death-borg config, onHit 0x800715f8 — directly confirms the 'slots identical, only callback differs' comparison.

shotVariantKinds.json corroboration beyond the cited entry: fireSitesByBorg key '0xc' lists exactly one site {table 0x802d6d68, id 5, fireFn zz_01a0458_, chunk_0049.c:2330}, and the extracted table metadata (stride 56, kindOffset 3, rowCount 10, rowFieldAddrs 0x802d6d68/6a/6b, dispatcher FUN_8007dcec via 0x802d6f98) all agree with the spawner decomp reads.
